<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967419 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05459: How aspects match with parts and ports

- page_id: `243967419`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967419/How+aspects+match+with+parts+and+ports
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:23:01.789+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Diagram aspects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The diagram aspects mechanism identifies whether or not the aspect name and name of the stereotypes applied for the parts and ports (or their types) match. The stereotype name must contain a matching aspect name. For example, a diagram aspect name is *Electrical,*which matches a port stereotype name W*Electrical.* When these conditions are met, you can display only the *Electrical* part of your model in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='']. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports according to aspects' space='']>]]>

If the diagram aspect name doesn't match the name of the part/port stereotype or its type, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MED'] with the same name as the aspect's and [apply it for parts and ports](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Stereotype#Stereotype-Applyingastereotype). Or you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating custom aspects' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Matching the diagram aspect *Electrical* with the port stereotype name *Electrical*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The diagram aspects mechanism identifies whether or not the aspect name and name of the stereotypes applied for the parts and ports (or their types) match. The stereotype name must contain a matching aspect name. For example, a diagram aspect name is <em>Electrical, </em>which matches a port stereotype name W<em>Electrical.</em> When these conditions are met, you can display only the <em>Electrical</em> part of your model in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Composite Structure Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports according to aspects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to display parts and port according to aspect >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4704f5b7-46ac-40ca-a191-e433fd556328"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the diagram aspect name doesn't match the name of the part/port stereotype or its type, you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a custom stereotype]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with the same name as the aspect's and <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Stereotype#Stereotype-Applyingastereotype">apply it for parts and ports</a>. Or you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating custom aspects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a custom diagram aspect]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="aspects_and_stereotype_match.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Matching the diagram aspect <em>Electrical</em> with the port stereotype name <em>Electrical</em>.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971343 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05460: How modeling tool adds its elements to velocity context

- page_id: `243971343`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971343/How+modeling+tool+adds+its+elements+to+velocity+context
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:11.924+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Template Creation Tutorial > Linking report templates to the modeling tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1358598954

1358598955

1358598955

In Velocity, whenever you want to reference to a Java object you need to manually add the object to the Velocity template and name it. In Report Wizard, however, this process is done automatically. What you need to understand is how it is done as it will help you to call the modeling tool elements.

All elements in the modeling tool such as Class, Use Case, and Activity are the sub-classes of elements. During report generation, the report engine will get all the elements from a project within a selected scope that you have defined in Report Wizard, and then classify and add them to a list of the same type. The list will then be added to the Velocity Context with its type as its name. For instance, in a project where there are two types of Element, Class, and Use Case, all the elements that are identified as Classes will be kept on the same list and the list will be added to the Velocity Context and labeled as "Class" during report generation. This process also applies to the Use Case elements. To reference to a specific Class element, first you will have to make a reference to **$Class** (list of Class elements), and then iterate through the list to find that particular Class element by using the **#foreach**, **#forrow**, **#forcol**, or **#forpage** directive, followed by the **#if** directive to search for the name of the class.

This section will use part of the "*Inventory Control System.mdzip*" file in *<install.root>/samples/casestudies* as an example to show you how to reference elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Use Case View.

You need to know the elements inside the "Use Case View" package before using it, see figure above. To do this, follow the instructions in the previous section, *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Your First Template' space='']*.

The "Use Case View" package contains three element types: (i) Actor, (ii) UseCase, and (iii) Diagram that you can call in a template by typing **"$Actor**," **"$UseCase**," and **"$Diagram**" respectively.

In this example you will create a report that will print the names of the Actors, UseCases, and diagram image.

1358598953

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

In Report Wizard, however, this process is done automatically. What you need to understand is how it is done as it will help you to call the modeling tool elements.</p><p>All elements in the modeling tool such as Class, Use Case, and Activity are the sub-classes of elements. During report generation, the report engine will get all the elements from a project within a selected scope that you have defined in Report Wizard, and then classify and add them to a list of the same type. The list will then be added to the Velocity Context with its type as its name. For instance, in a project where there are two types of Element, Class, and Use Case, all the elements that are identified as Classes will be kept on the same list and the list will be added to the Velocity Context and labeled as &quot;Class&quot; during report generation. This process also applies to the Use Case elements. To reference to a specific Class element, first you will have to make a reference to <strong>$Class</strong> (list of Class elements), and then iterate through the list to find that particular Class element by using the <strong>#foreach</strong>, <strong>#forrow</strong>, <strong>#forcol</strong>, or <strong>#forpage</strong> directive, followed by the <strong>#if</strong> directive to search for the name of the class.</p><p>This section will use part of the &quot;<em>Inventory Control System.mdzip</em>&quot; file in <em>&lt;install.root&gt;/samples/casestudies</em> as an example to show you how to reference elements.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Use Case and Actor" ac:alt="Use Case and Actor"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddavb053991ffb02adecec8c94bef76403bd.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;">Use Case View.</h6><p>You need to know the elements inside the &quot;Use Case View&quot; package before using it, see figure above. To do this, follow the instructions in the previous section, <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Your First Template" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating your first template]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em>.</p><p>The &quot;Use Case View&quot; package contains three element types: (i) Actor, (ii) UseCase, and (iii) Diagram that you can call in a template by typing <strong>&quot;$Actor</strong>,&quot; <strong>&quot;$UseCase</strong>,&quot; and <strong>&quot;$Diagram</strong>&quot; respectively.</p><p>In this example you will create a report that will print the names of the Actors, UseCases, and diagram image.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5863349b-5f1c-4533-8b27-111ca2bfbf72"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1358598953</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="8804a6f4-8982-4de8-b9e7-353a297adadb" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966178 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05461: How to avoid triggering element recovery

- page_id: `243966178`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966178/How+to+avoid+triggering+element+recovery
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:55.004+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Maintaining decomposed model integrity > Managing unresolved references
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following recommendations will help you avoid triggering element recovery in your project:

1. Avoid unexpected modifications of shared elements, such as removing a shared element or moving the element to a non-shared location, etc.
2. Configure the modeling tool to prompt you for confirmation before proceeding with a potentially dangerous action, such as attempting to stop sharing a package, removing the content of a shared package, or moving elements from one module to another, when both modules are used in the read-write mode (shown in the following figure).
3. Select the relevant level of model integrity and project composition inspection.
4. Manage unresolved references as soon as possible, as they might produce other unresolved references. Remember that the fewer recovered elements your model has, the more valid it is.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Examples of messages prompting to confirm potentially unsafe actions

To define a set of actions for modeling supervision to prevent recovered elements creation

1. Select Options > Projects to open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='']**Project Options** dialog .
2. In the option group list, select the General > Validation .
3. In the Validation options list under the Composition Inspection , set the Detect Illegal Model References in Shared Projects property value to true .
4. Click OK when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

inspection

To select the relevant level of project composition inspection

1. Select Options > Projects to open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='']**Project Options** dialog .
2. In the option group list, select the General > Validation .
3. In the Validation options list under the Composition Inspection , specify the Composition inspection option value:
  - Select Standard if you need the active validation to search for issues only in the project, but not in the projects it uses.
  - Select Advanced if you need the active validation to search for issues in the project as well as in the projects it uses.
4. Click OK when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The following recommendations will help you avoid triggering element recovery in your project:</p><ol><li>Avoid unexpected modifications of shared elements, such as removing a shared element or moving the element to a non-shared location, etc.</li><li>Configure the modeling tool to prompt you for confirmation before proceeding with a potentially dangerous action, such as attempting to stop sharing a package, removing the content of a shared package, or moving elements from one module to another, when both modules are used in the read-write mode (shown in the following figure).</li><li>Select the relevant level of model integrity and project composition inspection.</li><li>Manage unresolved references as soon as possible, as they might produce other unresolved references. Remember that the fewer recovered elements your model has, the more valid it is.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="avoid_recovery.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Examples of messages prompting to confirm potentially unsafe actions</h6><p>To define a set of actions for modeling supervision to prevent recovered elements creation</p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Projects</strong> to open the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>In the option group list, select the <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>Validation</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Validation </strong>options list under the <strong> Composition Inspection</strong>, set the <strong>Detect Illegal Model References in Shared Projects </strong>property value to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="detect_illegal_model_references_in_shared_projects.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2eb30e2f-6a0b-40da-97d1-2126e8b29b05"><ac:parameter ac:name="">inspection</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p>To select the relevant level of project composition inspection</p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Projects</strong> to open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li style="text-align: left;">In the option group list, select the <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>Validation</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Validation </strong>options list under the <strong>Composition Inspection</strong>, specify the <strong>Composition inspection</strong> option value:<ul><li>Select <strong>Standard</strong> if you need the active validation to search for issues <em>only</em> in the project, but not in the projects it uses.</li><li>Select <strong>Advanced</strong> if you need the active validation to search for issues in the project as well as in the projects it uses.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565437 space=MT version=5 -->
## PAGE 05462: How to migrate

- page_id: `249565437`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565437/How+to+migrate
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:57:37.576+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before migrating your projects to the latest profile versions, we strongly advise you to make a backup first. After the migration is completed, you will not be able to reverse it.

For more information about migrating server projects, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migrating projects' space='CM'].

.

If you agree to migrate the project, an auto-check of the project usage is performed. You are warned if there are used projects of incompatible versions that, you need to convert. Two types of usages can interrupt migration:

- Cyclic usage
- Indirect usage

If the project contains cyclic usage, you will receivea warning with those usages listed. To continue with the migration, you need to eliminate the listed cyclic usages first. Repeat the migration.

For information about how to identify cyclic usages, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying suspicious usages' space='MCS']. The cyclic usages should be solved with the version you worked on before the migration.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After cyclic usages are eliminated, and you continue with the migration, the check for indirect usages will be performed.

If the main project contains indirectly used projects, you will receive a warning message listing those projects. To continue with the migration, you need to migrate the indirectly used projects one by one **in the same order they are listed in the message**. Repeat the migration.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After the cyclic and indirect usages are solved, you are informed about the directly used projects of incompatible versions. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Project+migration+issues+on+Teamwork+Cloud#ProjectmigrationissuesonTeamworkCloud-Usedprojectsaremigrated,butnotupdated)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

More about migrating UAF and UPDM projects:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF projects migration manual' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UPDM projects migration manual' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<div><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cee6011b-0cd8-4f2d-89d1-cd8d071c707f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Before migrating your projects to the latest profile versions, we strongly advise you to make a backup first. After the migration is completed, you will not be able to reverse it.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="015ab40b-16ae-4330-a8ed-746e3ac71725"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about migrating server projects, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CM" ri:content-title="Migrating projects" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><div>You are suggested to migrate each time you open a project created with the older version of UPDM / UAF with the modeling tool with the latest UAF version<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9e05a89f-f8c4-440f-b94f-84f299446569"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="167a9859-8516-48fb-a158-b784804c3e6c">.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><br /></div><div><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9e05a89f-f8c4-440f-b94f-84f299446569"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="167a9859-8516-48fb-a158-b784804c3e6c"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="migration_question_new.png.jpg" /></ac:image></ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></div><div><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If you agree to migrate the project, an auto-check of the project usage is performed. You are warned if there are used projects of incompatible versions that, you need to convert. Two types of usages can interrupt migration:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Cyclic usage</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Indirect usage</span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If the project contains cyclic usage, you will receive </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">a warning with those usages listed. To continue with the migration, you need to eliminate the listed cyclic usages first. Repeat the migration.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4eeac4a9-7b3f-4916-b856-df9bd9a159ec"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For information about how to identify cyclic usages, see <ac:link ac:anchor="Cyclic Usages"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Identifying suspicious usages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cyclic Usages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The cyclic usages should be solved with the version you worked on before the migration.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="warning cycle.PNG" /></ac:image></p><p>After cyclic usages are eliminated, and you continue with the migration, the check for indirect usages will be performed.</p><p>If the main project contains indirectly used projects, you will receive a warning message listing those projects. To continue with the migration, you need to migrate the indirectly used projects one by one <u><strong>in the same order they are listed in the message</strong></u>. Repeat the migration.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="question2.PNG" /></ac:image></p><p>After the cyclic and indirect usages are solved, you are informed about the directly used projects of incompatible versions. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Project+migration+issues+on+Teamwork+Cloud#ProjectmigrationissuesonTeamworkCloud-Usedprojectsaremigrated,butnotupdated">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="263" ac:width="807"><ri:attachment ri:filename="pic2.png" /></ac:image></p><p>  </p><div><p><br /></p></div><div><p class="auto-cursor-target">More about migrating UAF and UPDM projects:</p></div><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UAF projects migration manual" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UPDM projects migration manual" /></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968461 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05463: HTML editor

- page_id: `243968461`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968461/HTML+editor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:59.639+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

630043998

630044000

630043999

You can easily add or edit HTML text using the HTML editor. It also allows you to preserve the text format when copying formatted text.

To set the note, text box, or separator text as HTML

Do one of the following:

- Select a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Note' space='MED'] / [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Text Box' space=''] shape and click the HTML button when Switch To HTML Text appears on the lower-left corner of the shape. [ATTACHMENT filename='switch_text_to_html.png']

- Draw the note/text box, using the Note(HTML text) or Text Box(HTML text) buttons on the diagram palette.

Click the text area on the selected shape or start typing to open the HTML editor toolbar. The buttons on the HTML editor toolbar have the same functions as a normal editing toolbar.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']HTML editor toolbar

You can write HTML text in various dialogs, but you must turn on the HTML mode first.

To turn on the HTML mode in a dialog

- Simply click to select the HTML check box. The following scenarios show the various ways of reaching the check box:
  - Element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] :
    1. Open an element's specification window.
    2. Click the Documentation/Comments tab.
    3. Check HTML. Click Close . See the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### HTML editor toolbar in Documentation/Hyperlinks tab of element Specification window

- In the Model Browser:
  1. Click the element in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment Tree' space='MED'] .
  2. Click the **Documentation** tab.
  3. Click **HTML**, then click anywhere on the diagram pane. See the figure below. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] HTML editor toolbar in Documentation panel on Model Browser
- Element Specification window:
  1. Open an element specification window.
  2. Click the Tags property group, when editing a tagged value.
  3. Click HTML . Then click Close . See the figure below. [ATTACHMENT filename='customer_user_is_added.png'] HTML editor toolbar in Tags tab of element Specification window when editing tag value
- In the **To Do** field:
  1. Open an element specification window.
  2. In the dialog, click the**To Do** field to edit it.
  3. Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to open the **To Do** text dialog.
  4. In the **To Do** text dialog, click **HTML**. Edit the text as you wish, then click **OK** and click **Close**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']HTML editor toolbar in To Do dialog

You can also use the HTML editor toolbar when editing a tagged value directly on the element shape.

To use the HTML editor toolbar for editing a tagged value

1. Click the HTML button on the element shape to switch to the HTML mode.
2. On the element shape, click the tagged value. The HTML editor toolbar opens for editing the tagged value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

630043997

**Related Pages:**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562104 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 05464: Humidifier example to Simulink model

- page_id: `249562104`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562104/Humidifier+example+to+Simulink+model
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-25T11:55:39.492+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Exporting to External Simulation Models > Simulink export > Simulating exported Simulink models
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page describes how to simulate the *Humidifier*example model in Simulink. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Humidifier system sample' space='']Learn more about *Humidifier*sample model >>

You can find the *Humidifier*sample model in:

- the modeling tool: Welcome window > Samples > Simulink and Modelica Transformation > Humidifier.
- the Installation directory: <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1\Simulink and Modelica Transformation\*Humidifier**.*

To simulate the *Humidifier System* model

1. Export the HumidifierSystem Block to Simulink file. How to >>
2. In the Simulink Export Options dialog select the following options:
  - **Format**: XML (.slx)
  - **S-Function or Simscape**: S-Function version 2
  - Composite Signals : Bus Creators/Selectors
3. Make sure the MATLAB tool is installed.
4. Double-click the MATLAB icon to start it.
5. In the file directory select the location of your exported Humidifier Simulink files. They are displayed in the Current Folder panel. [ATTACHMENT filename='file_directory_simulink_files.png']
6. Double-click the file HumidifierSystem.slx . The model open in the Simulink window. [ATTACHMENT filename='simulink_window.png']
7. On the SIMULATION tab, click [ATTACHMENT filename='library_browser_button.png'] .
8. In the Simulink Library Browser dialog, select expand the Simulink group and select Sinks . See the figure below.
9. Drag and drop the Scope block onto the HumidifierSystem Simulink model. See the figure below.
10. Select signal line that connects the humidity-out port of the room block to the humidity-in port of the humidifier block and click on the Scope block. See the figure below. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_scope_block.png']
11. On the SIMULATION tab, click the arrow on the PREPARE area and select the Model Settings . [ATTACHMENT filename='model_settings.png']
12. In the Configuration Parameters dialog, on the left side of the dialog select a Solver and specify the following on the right side of the dialog: - In the the Simulation time group change Start time to 0.0 , Stop time to 3600 . - In the Solver selection group select Type Variable-step or any other desirable solver that is suitable. - Click the arrow of the Solver details group and change the Max step size to 0.1 . [ATTACHMENT filename='configuration_parameters_dialog.png']
13. Press Apply > OK .
14. Double-click the Scope block in the model. An empty black plot opens.
15. Click button. In the Configuration Properties: Scope dialog, select the Logging tab and clear the Limit data points to last box. [ATTACHMENT filename='configuration_properties.png']
16. Press Apply > OK .
17. In the Scope plot toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and wait for the simulation results in the plot. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562127 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 05465: Humidifier example to Simulink model R2016a

- page_id: `249562127`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562127/Humidifier+example+to+Simulink+model+R2016a
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:55:58.200+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Exporting to External Simulation Models > Simulink export > Simulating exported Simulink models > Humidifier example to Simulink model
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To generate a Simulink model directly from the modeling tool

1. Right-click the Block TestCaseHumidifier::HumidifierSystem and select Tools > Export to Simulink.
2. Set the options listed below and click OK :
  - **Format**: XML (.sdl)
  - **S-Function or Simscape**: S-Function version 2
3. Launch Matlab with Simulink and Simscape extensions.
4. In Matlab’s Current Directory navigation bar, search for the file directory where the Simulink files (as well as the SysML v1 files) are saved, and set it as the current directory. The Current Folder panel should display two generated Simulink “.slx” files. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. In the Current Folder panel, double-click on the file named Environment.slx . The model will open up in a Simulink window. Rearrange the blocks to reflect the signal flow arrowhead directions. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Open the Library Browser either by using the 4-block icon or by going to Tools > Library Browser . Find and click on Sinks among the list in the Libraries panel, which will be categorized under Simulink . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Pull-and-drag the Scope block from the Sinks library list into the Environment Simulink model. Attach a signal line segment from the Scope port onto the signal line that connects the humidity-out port of the room block to the humidity-in port of the humidifiersystem block. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. Go to Simulation > Model Configuration Settings . This allows selecting the types of solver and the runtime of the simulation. Select a Start Time of 0 , and a Stop Time of 3600 . Under Solver Options , select the solver Type to Variable-step (or any other desirable solver that is suitable) and change the Max step size to 0.01 . Press Apply , then press OK . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. Double-click the Scope block in the model. An empty (black) plot will show up. Press the ‘Scope’ parameters button (the gear icon above the empty plot). A ‘Scope’ parameters dialogue box will pop up. Go to the History tab and uncheck the option box Limit data points to last . Press Apply , and then OK . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
10. Go to Simulation > Run . Double-click on the Scope block to see the simulation results. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Under <strong>Solver Options</strong>, select the solver<strong> Type</strong> to <strong>Variable-step</strong> (or any other desirable solver that is suitable) and change the <strong>Max step size</strong> to <strong>0.01</strong>. Press<strong> Apply</strong>, then press<strong> OK</strong>.<br /><br /><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"><ac:image ac:width="750"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Simulink_Model_Configuration_Settings.png" /></ac:image></span></li><li>Double-click the <strong>Scope</strong> block in the model. An empty (black) plot will show up. Press the <strong>‘Scope’ parameters</strong> button (the gear icon above the empty plot). A <strong>‘Scope’ parameters</strong> dialogue box will pop up. Press <strong>Apply</strong>, and then <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><br /><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"><ac:image ac:width="700"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Configuring_the_Scope.png" /></ac:image></span></li><li>Go to <strong>Simulation</strong> &gt; <strong>Run</strong>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562234 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05466: Humidifier System example

- page_id: `249562234`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562234/Humidifier+System+example
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-25T11:50:03.589+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Exporting to External Simulation Models > Modelica export > Simulating exported Modelica models
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page describes how to simulate the *Humidifier*example model in Dymola. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Humidifier system sample' space='']Learn more about *Humidifier*sample model >>

You can find the *Humidifier*sample model in:

- the modeling tool: Welcome window > Samples > Simulink and Modelica Transformation > Humidifier.
- the Installation directory: <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1\Simulink and Modelica Transformation\Humidifier.

To simulate the *Humidifier System* model

1. Export the HumidifierSystem Block to the Modelica file. To learn more, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modelica export' space=''] page.
2. Download and install Dymole in your system. To learn more, refer to the Dymola page.
3. Launch the Dymola.
4. Click File > Open > Load... and select the saved *HumidifierSystem**.mo* file from your directory. The Humidifier System model is displayed in the **Package Browser**. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. In the Graphics tab toolbar, click Check to check for syntax and semantic errors in the model. The Logs panel displays whether the model is ready for simulation. [ATTACHMENT filename='Check Button.png']
6. Setup the simulation run for the model: 
 a. Select the******Simulation**tab and in its toolbar click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. 
b. In the **Simulation Setup** dialog, specify the **Start time******value to **0** and the **Stop time******value to**3600**seconds. 
c. Click **OK**. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. In the**Simulation** tab toolbar, click **Simulate**. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] When the simulation is completed, the output is displayed in the panel at the bottom and the *HumidifierSystem*variables are displayed in the **Variable Browser**.
8. To see the input and output of the EnvironmentModel , variables from the Variable Browser must be chosen. Selecting EnvironmentModel > _Environment > room > humidity_out will display the humidity level of the room overtime on the plotting window.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This page describes how to simulate the <em>Humidifier </em>example model in Dymola. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Humidifier system sample" /><ac:link-body>Learn more about <em>Humidifier </em>sample model &gt;&gt;</ac:link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="92b70d06-6f5d-4d3c-aca6-61891a66027f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can f<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">ind the <em>Humidifier </em>sample model in:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the modeling tool: Welcome window &gt; Samples &gt; Simulink and Modelica Transformation &gt; Humidifier.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the Installation directory: </span><em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\SysML v1\Simulink and Modelica Transformation\Humidifier.</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To simulate the <em>Humidifier System</em> model</p><hr /><ol><li>Export the <em>HumidifierSystem </em>Block to the Modelica file. To learn more, refer to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Modelica export" /></ac:link> page.</li><li>Download and install Dymole in your system. To learn more, refer to the <a href="https://www.3ds.com/products-services/catia/products/dymola/?woc=%7B%22category%22%3A%5B%22category%2Fdymola%22%5D%7D&amp;wockw=card_content_cta_1_url%3A%22https%3A%2F%2Fblogs.3ds.com%2Fcatia%2F%22">Dymola</a> page.</li><li>Launch the Dymola.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt;</span><strong> Open </strong>&gt;<strong> Load... </strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">and select the saved <em>HumidifierSystem</em><em>.mo</em> file from your directory. The Humidifier System model is displayed in the <strong>Package Browser</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Package Browser.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li>In the Graphics tab toolbar, click <strong>Check </strong>to check for syntax and semantic errors in the model. The <strong>Logs </strong>panel displays whether the model is ready for simulation.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Check Button.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Setup the simulation run for the model:<br />    a. Select the<strong> </strong><strong>Simulation </strong>tab and in its toolbar click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="setup_button.png" /></ac:image>.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    b. In the <strong>Simulation Setup</strong> dialog, specify the <strong>Start time</strong><strong> </strong>value to <strong>0</strong> and the <strong>Stop time</strong><strong> </strong>value to<strong> 3600 </strong>seconds.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    c. Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Simulatio Setup.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span></span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the<strong> Simulation</strong> tab toolbar, click <strong>Simulate</strong>. <br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Simulate Button.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c0ad6689-6aa0-43a3-b678-89d3d32e2be3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When the simulation is completed, the output is displayed in the panel at the bottom and the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><em>HumidifierSystem </em>variables are displayed in the <strong>Variable Browser</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To see the input and output of the</span><strong> EnvironmentModel</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, variables from the </span><strong>Variable Browser</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> must be chosen. Selecting </span><strong>EnvironmentModel</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>_Environment</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>room</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>humidity_out</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> will display the humidity level of the room overtime on the plotting window.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562228 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 05468: Hydraulics example

- page_id: `249562228`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562228/Hydraulics+example
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-25T11:50:18.853+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Exporting to External Simulation Models > Modelica export > Simulating exported Modelica models
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page describes how to simulate the *Hydraulics* example model in Dymola. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hydraulics sample' space='']Learn more about *Hydraulics*sample model >>

You can find the *Hydraulics*sample model in:

- the modeling tool: Welcome window > Samples > Simulink and Modelica Transformation > Hydraulics .
- the Installation directory: <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1\Simulink and Modelica Transformation\*Hydraulics**.*

To simulate the *ConnectedTanks* model

1. Export the ConnectedTanks Block to Modelica file. How to >>
2. Make sure the Dymola tool is installed. How to >>
3. Start the Dymola.
4. Click File > Open > Load... and select saved ConnectedTanks .mo file from your file directory. A ConnectedTanks model is displayed in the Package Browser . [ATTACHMENT filename='connectedtanks_model_in_package_browser.png']
5. In the Graphics tab toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='check_syntax_button.png'] to check model for syntax and semantic errors. The result panel displays whether the model is ready for simulation. [ATTACHMENT filename='check_syntax_connectedTanks.png']
6. Setup the simulation run for the model: a. Select the Simulation tab and in its toolbar click [ATTACHMENT filename='setup_button.png'] . b. In the Simulation Setup dialog, specify the Start time value to 0 and the Stop time value to 120 seconds (or any other reasonable amount of time; for this model, 120 seconds should be enough) . c. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='simulation_setup_connectedTanks.png']
7. In the**Simulation** tab toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. When the simulation is completed, the output is displayed in the panel at the bottom and the *ConnectedTanks***variables are displayed in the **Variable Browser**.
8. To see the input and output of the ConntectedTanks model , select variables in the**Variable Browser**as follows: - ConnectedTanks > _ConnectedTanks > fluidReservoir1 > fluidLevel . The changing fluid level of the first tank is displayed on the **Plot** window. See the following figure. - ConnectedTanks > _ConnectedTanks > fluidReservoir2 > fluidLevel . Thechanging fluid level of the second tank is displayed on the **Plot**window. See the following figure. [ATTACHMENT filename='plot_diagram_fluid_level.png']

In the <strong>Simulation Setup</strong> dialog, specify the <strong>Start time</strong><strong> </strong>value to <strong>0</strong> and the <strong>Stop time</strong><strong> </strong>value to<strong> 120 </strong>seconds <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">(or any other reasonable amount of time; for this model, 120 seconds should be enough)</span> .<br />    c. The<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> changing fluid level of the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">first tank is displayed</span> on the <strong>Plot</strong> window. See the following figure.<br /></span><strong> </strong>-<strong> ConnectedTanks</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>_ConnectedTanks</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>fluidReservoir2</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt;</span><strong> fluidLevel</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. The<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> changing fluid level of the second tank is displayed on the <strong>Plot </strong>window. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">See the following figure.</span></span></span><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="plot_diagram_fluid_level.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562048 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 05469: Hydraulics example to Simulink and Simscape model

- page_id: `249562048`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562048/Hydraulics+example+to+Simulink+and+Simscape+model
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-25T11:56:36.636+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Exporting to External Simulation Models > Simulink export > Simulating exported Simulink models
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page describes how to simulate the *Hydraulics* example modelin Simulink. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hydraulics sample' space='']Learn more about *Hydraulics*sample model >>

You can find the *Hydraulics*sample model in:

- the modeling tool: Welcome window > Samples > Simulink and Modelica Transformation > Hydraulics .
- the Installation directory: <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1\Simulink and Modelica Transformation\*Hydraulics**.*

To simulate the *Hydraulics* model

1. Export the ConnectedTanks Block to Simulink file. How to >>
2. In the Simulink Export Options dialog select the following options:
  - **Format**: XML (.slx)
  - **S-Function or Simscape**: Simscape
  - Simscape port libraries : Create new port types
  - Composite Signals : Bus Creators/Selectors
3. Make sure the MATLAB tool is installed.
4. Double-click the MATLAB icon to start it.
5. In the file directory select the location of your exported *Hydraulics* model Simulink files and choose the +ConnectedTanksLibrary folder. The simscape files (.ssc) are displayed in the Current Folder panel. [ATTACHMENT filename='file_directory_simulink_files_connectedTanks.png'] ssc_build
6. In the Command Window type ssc_build (or sscbuild depending on the Matlab version) and press Enter . This generates a folder sscprj in the +**ConnectedTanksLibrary** directory as well as a **ConnectedTanksLibrary**_lib.slx file in the directory with the original ConnectedTanks .slx file. [ATTACHMENT filename='command_window_connectedTanks.png']
7. Go back to the file directory, select the Hydraulics package with exported Simulink files and in the Current Folder select the ConnectedTanks.slx . [ATTACHMENT filename='current_folder_connectedTanks.png']
8. In the Current Folder panel, double-click on ConnectedTanks .slx to open the Simulink/Simscape model. Rearrange the blocks to get a better picture of the blocks and links. [ATTACHMENT filename='hydraulics_model_simscape.png']
9. On the **SIMULATION** tab, click the arrow on the **PREPARE** area and select the **Model Settings**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
10. In the **Configuration Parameters** dialog, on the left side of the dialog: 
a. Select a **Solver**property group****and specify the following on the right side of the dialog: 
****-****In the the **Simulation time** group, change **Start time**to **0.0**, **Stop time**to **200**(or any other reasonable number of seconds). 
 - In the **Solver selection** group, select**Type****Variable-step** or any other desirable solver that is suitable. 
 - Click the arrow of the **Solver details** group and change the **Max step size** to **0.01**. b. Select Simscape property group and specify the following on the right side of the dialog: - In the Data Logging group, change Log simulation data to All . - Make sure Limit data points is unchecked. [ATTACHMENT filename='configuration_parameters_dialog_connectedTanks.png']
11. Press Apply > OK .
12. In the ConnectedTanks Simulink window, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
13. On the main toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open a Simscape Results Explorer : ConnectedTanks window where you can expand the ConnectedTanks blocks to see how their properties simulated through time. For the tanks fluidReservoir1 and fluidReservoir2 , the properties fluidLevel can be selected to see how the fluid reacted transferred from one tank to the other in the simulation. To see the simulation’s specific data points, select the mouse pointer on the plot. [ATTACHMENT filename='simscape_results_explorer_connectedTanks.png']
14. To change the default value or initial value parameters of the model, double-click the blocks in the ConnectedTanks Simulink model. A Block Parameters dialog opens where you can change parameters. Repeat steps from 5 to 12 to run the simulation with (new) block parameters. [ATTACHMENT filename='fluiReservoir2_block_prameters.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

This generates a folder <strong>sscprj</strong> in the <strong>+<strong>ConnectedTanksLibrary </strong></strong>directory as well as a<strong> <strong>ConnectedTanksLibrary</strong>_lib.slx</strong> file in the directory with the original <strong>ConnectedTanks</strong><strong>.slx</strong> file.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="command_window_connectedTanks.png" /></ac:image></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Go back to the file directory, </span>select the <em>Hydraulics </em>package with exported Simulink files and in the <strong>Current Folder</strong> select the <em>ConnectedTanks.slx</em>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="current_folder_connectedTanks.png" /></ac:image></li><li>In the <strong>Current Folder</strong> panel, double-click on <strong>ConnectedTanks</strong><strong>.slx</strong> to open the Simulink/Simscape model. Rearrange the blocks to get a better picture of the blocks and links.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="hydraulics_model_simscape.png" /></ac:image></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">On the <strong>SIMULATION</strong> tab, click the arrow on the <strong>PREPARE</strong> area and select the <strong>Model Settings</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_settings.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Configuration Parameters</strong> dialog, on the left side of the dialog:<br />a. Select a  <strong>Solver </strong>property group<strong> </strong>and specify the following on the right side of the dialog:<br /><strong>  </strong>-<strong> </strong>In the the <strong>Simulation time</strong> group, change <strong>Start time </strong>to <strong>0.0</strong>, <strong>Stop time </strong>to <strong>200 </strong>(or any other reasonable number of seconds).<br />  - In the <strong>Solver selection</strong> group, select<strong> Type </strong><strong>Variable-step</strong> or any other desirable solver that is suitable.<br />  - Click the arrow of the <strong>Solver details</strong> group and change the <strong>Max step size</strong> to <strong>0.01</strong>.<br /></span>b. Select <strong>Simscape</strong> property group <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">and specify the following on the right side of the dialog:<br /></span>  - In the <strong>Data Logging</strong> group, change <strong>Log simulation data</strong> to <strong>All</strong>.<br />  - Make sure <strong>Limit data points</strong> is unchecked.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="configuration_parameters_dialog_connectedTanks.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Press<strong> Apply</strong> &gt;<strong> OK</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>ConnectedTanks</strong> Simulink window, click<strong> <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="24"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Run_button_matlab.png" /></ac:image>.</strong></li><li>On the main toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="64"><ri:attachment ri:filename="simscape_results_button.png" /></ac:image></span> to open a <strong>Simscape Results Explorer</strong>: <strong>ConnectedTanks </strong>window where you can expand the  <strong>ConnectedTanks </strong>blocks to see how their properties simulated through time. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">For the tanks </span><strong>fluidReservoir1</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> and</span><strong> fluidReservoir2</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, the properties </span><strong>fluidLevel</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> can be selected to see how the fluid reacted transferred from one tank to the other in the simulation.</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To see the simulation’s specific data points, select the mouse pointer on the plot.</span><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="simscape_results_explorer_connectedTanks.png" /></ac:image></li><li>To change the default value or initial value parameters of the model, double-click the blocks in the<strong> ConnectedTanks Simulink</strong> model. A <strong>Block Parameters</strong> dialog opens where you can change parameters. Repeat steps from 5 to 12 to run the simulation with (new) block parameters.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="fluiReservoir2_block_prameters.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></div><p><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562075 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 05470: Hydraulics example to Simulink and Simscape model R2016a

- page_id: `249562075`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562075/Hydraulics+example+to+Simulink+and+Simscape+model+R2016a
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:55:38.109+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Exporting to External Simulation Models > Simulink export > Simulating exported Simulink models > Hydraulics example to Simulink and Simscape model
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To generate a Simulink model directly from the modeling tool

1. Right-click the *Block* **Data:ConnectedTanks** and select **Tools**> **Export to Simulink.**
2. Set the options listed below and click **OK**:
  - **Format**: XML (.sdl)
  - **S-Function or Simscape**: Simscape
  - **Simscape port libraries**: Create new port types
3. Launch Matlab with Simulink and Simscape extensions.
4. In Matlab’s Current Directory navigation bar, search for the file directory where the Simulink/Simscape files (as well as the SysML v1 files) are saved, and set it as the current directory. The Current Folder panel should display the generated files.
5. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. In the **Current Folder** panel, double-click on the folder named**+ConnectedTanksLibrary_create** (this sets the current directory to this folder). Type **ssc_build** in the**Command Window** and press Enter. This will generate a folder **sscprj** in the **+ConnectedTanksLibrary_create** directory as well as a **ConnectedTanksLibrary_create_lib.slx** file in the directory with the original **ConnectedTanks.slx** file. Next, once again, search for and go back to the file directory where the Simulink/Simscape files (as well as the SysML files) are saved, and set it as the current directory. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. In the **Current Folder** panel, double-click on **ConnectedTanks.slx** to open the Simulink/Simscape model. Rearrange the blocks to get a better picture of the blocks and links. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. Go to **Simulation** > **Model Configuration Settings**. This allows to select the types of solver and the runtime of the simulation. Select a **Start Time** of **0**, and a **Stop Time** of **200** (or any other reasonable number of seconds). Change the **Max step size** to **0.01**. Under **Solver Options**, keep the solver **Type** to **Variable-step** (or any other desirable solver that is suitable). Press **Apply**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. In Model Configuration Settings, select **Simscape** in the**Commonly Used Parameters** panel. Change **Log simulation data** to **All**. Make sure**Limit data points**is unchecked. Press **Apply**. Press **OK**. [ATTACHMENT filename='Model_Configuration_Settings.png']
10. In the ConnectedTanks Simulink window, go to Simulation > Run .
11. Once the model has compiled, go to the Matlab’s command window and type/enter ssc_explore(simlog) (make sure that either the version of Matlab has the function ssc_explore , or a file ssc_explore.m is in the same directory as ConnectedTanks.slx in the Current Folder panel). A Simscape data logging explorer: Connected Tanks window will open up. [ATTACHMENT filename='Ssc_explore(simlog)_command_and_the_Simscape_data_logging_explorer.png']
12. In the Simscape data logging explorer , the ConnectedTanks blocks can be expanded to see how their properties simulated through time. For the tanks fluidReservoir1 and fluidReservoir2 , the properties fluidLevel can be selected to see how the fluid reacted transferred from one tank to the other in the simulation. To see the simulation’s specific data points, the Data cursor icon needs to be selected and points on the plot need to be chosen (by clicking it). [ATTACHMENT filename='Using_the_Simscape_data_loggng_explorer.png']
13. To change the default value or initial value parameters of the model, double-click the blocks in the ConnectedTanks Simulink model. A window will pop out for block parameters that can be changed. Run through Steps 7 through 10 to run the simulation with (new) block parameters again. [ATTACHMENT filename='Changing_block_parameters.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><br /></p><p>To generate a Simulink model directly from the modeling tool </p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Right-click the <em>Block</em> <strong>Data:ConnectedTanks</strong> and select <strong>Tools </strong>&gt; <strong>Export to Simulink.</strong></span><br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Set the options listed below and click <strong>OK</strong>:</span><ul><li><p><strong>Format</strong>: XML (.sdl)</p></li><li><p><strong>S-Function or Simscape</strong>: Simscape</p></li><li><p><strong>Simscape port libraries</strong>: Create new port types</p></li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Launch Matlab with Simulink and Simscape extensions. </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In Matlab’s</span><strong> Current Directory</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> navigation bar, search for the file directory where the Simulink/Simscape files (as well as the SysML v1 files) are saved, and set it as the current directory. The </span><strong>Current Folder</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> panel should display the generated files. </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:width="800"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Change_Directory_in_Matlab.png" /></ac:image><br /></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Current Folder</strong> panel, double-click on the folder named<strong> +ConnectedTanksLibrary_create</strong> (this sets the current directory to this folder). Next, once again, search for and go back to the file directory where the Simulink/Simscape files (as well as the SysML files) are saved, and set it as the current directory.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:width="800"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Using_the_Scc_Build_Command.png" /></ac:image><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Current Folder</strong> panel, double-click on <strong>ConnectedTanks.slx</strong> to open the Simulink/Simscape model. Rearrange the blocks to get a better picture of the blocks and links.<br /></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ConnectedTanks_Simulink_Simscape_Diagram.png" /></ac:image><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Go to <strong>Simulation</strong> &gt; <strong>Model Configuration Settings</strong>. This allows to select the types of solver and the runtime of the simulation. Select a <strong>Start Time</strong> of <strong>0</strong>, and a <strong>Stop Time</strong> of <strong>200</strong> (or any other reasonable number of seconds). Change the <strong>Max step size</strong> to <strong>0.01</strong>. Under <strong>Solver Options</strong>, keep the solver <strong>Type</strong> to <strong>Variable-step</strong> (or any other desirable solver that is suitable). Press <strong>Apply</strong>.<br /></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:width="800"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Simulink_Simscape_Model_Configuration_Settings.png" /></ac:image><br /></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In Model Configuration Settings, select <strong>Simscape</strong> in the<strong> Commonly Used Parameters</strong> panel. Change <strong>Log simulation data</strong> to <strong>All</strong>. Make sure<strong> Limit data points </strong>is unchecked. Press <strong>Apply</strong>. Press <strong>OK</strong>.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Model_Configuration_Settings.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>In the <strong>ConnectedTanks</strong> Simulink window, go to <strong>Simulation</strong> &gt; <strong>Run</strong>.</li><li>Once the model has compiled, go to the Matlab’s command window and type/enter<strong> ssc_explore(simlog)</strong> (make sure that either the version of Matlab has the function <strong>ssc_explore</strong>, or a file <strong>ssc_explore.m</strong> is in the same directory as <strong>ConnectedTanks.slx</strong> in the <strong>Current Folder</strong> panel). A <strong>Simscape data logging explorer: Connected Tanks</strong> window will open up.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="650"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Ssc_explore(simlog)_command_and_the_Simscape_data_logging_explorer.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>In the <strong>Simscape data logging explorer</strong>, the <strong>ConnectedTanks</strong> blocks can be expanded to see how their properties simulated through time. For the tanks <strong>fluidReservoir1</strong> and<strong> fluidReservoir2</strong>, the properties <strong>fluidLevel</strong> can be selected to see how the fluid reacted transferred from one tank to the other in the simulation. To see the simulation’s specific data points, the <strong>Data cursor</strong> icon needs to be selected and points on the plot need to be chosen (by clicking it).<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Using_the_Simscape_data_loggng_explorer.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>To change the default value or initial value parameters of the model, double-click the blocks in the <strong>ConnectedTanks</strong> Simulink model. A window will pop out for block parameters that can be changed. Run through Steps 7 through 10 to run the simulation with (new) block parameters again.<br /><ac:image ac:width="750"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Changing_block_parameters.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968380 space=MT version=12 -->
## PAGE 05472: Hyperlinks in texts

- page_id: `243968380`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968380/Hyperlinks+in+texts
- version_number: 12
- version_date: `2025-09-11T19:48:43.437+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Manage Navigation > Defining hyperlinks
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can link text directly to internal or external resources by adding hyperlinks. This enables you to open a document, wizard, diagram, or other [hyperlink type](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Edit+Hyperlink+dialog#EditHyperlinkdialog-Hyperlinktypes) by clicking the hyperlink.

##### Places to add hyperlink in text

You can add a hyperlink in the following places:

- In all elements of the text format (e.g. Note, Anchor, Comment).
- In all element property values of the text format (e.g. **To Do**, **Documentation**, **Body**, **Body** **and Language**, **Text**,**Min**,**Max**). You can insert a hyperlink only for LiteralString and OpaqueExpression [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Specification' space='MED'] (e.g **Default Value** property, **Value** property, and **Argument** property values).
- In all element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] > Documentation/Hyperlinks property groups.

##### Adding hyperlinks in text

Before adding a hyperlink, select the text format, either HTML or Plain text. If you select:

- HTML text - you can add any type of hyperlink . >]]>
- Plain text - you can add a hyperlink only to an element/symbol. >]]>

###### all typesAdding a hyperlink in text to any hyperlink type

To add a hyperlink in text to any hyperlink type

1. Select a place to add a hyperlink. [All places to add hyperlinks in text >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Hyperlinks+in+texts#Hyperlinksintexts-Placestoinserthyperlinkintext)
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='insert_hyperlink.png'] or press Ctrl+K.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Edit Hyperlink dialog' space=''] , select the tab according to hyperlink type.
4. Specify the boxes and options in the selected tab. Descriptions of boxes and tabsPlease see the following for more in-depth information about the boxes and options on each tab: 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Address tab' space='']**Address** tab. 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='File tab' space='']**File** tab. 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element Symbol tab' space='']**Element/Symbol** tab. 
 - [**OSLC Link** tab.](https://docs.nomagic.com/CDH/?contextKey=linking-model-elements-to-oslc-resources&version=latest)
5. Click **OK**.The hyperlink added in text becomes underlined and blue. For example, the figure below shows where the hyperlink on the text "Car" will open the Specification window of the *Car* Class. The hyperlinked text will be represented with icon and will be automatically updated after changes. [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Element/Symbol tab in the Edit Hyperlink dialog where the text "Car" refers to the Car Class from the model. It is represented with an icon and will be automatically updated after changes.

###### elementAdding hyperlink in text to element/symbol

Two additional ways to add a hyperlink in text to an element/symbol include:

- 
- 

****

###### **drag**Adding hyperlink to element by dragging element on Note or Text box shape

This method of adding hyperlinks in text is useful only when inserting a hyperlink to element on Note or Text box shapes.

To add a hyperlink to an element by dragging an element on a Note or Text box shape

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] , select one or more elements.
2. Drag the selected elements onto the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Note' space='MED'] or the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Text Box' space=''] shapes on the diagram pane. 
The hyperlink to the element Specification window is created on the Note or Text Box. The text become underlined and blue. The **Update Mode** and **Text to display** values are selected according default settings. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Default settings of hyperlinks to model elements' space='']How to change the default settings of hyperlinks to model elements >>

###### **guards**Adding hyperlinks to elements on Guard expressions, Change Event expressions, and State Invariant

This method of adding hyperlinks in text is useful when inserting hyperlinks to properties on:

- A Transition Guard in a State Machine diagram.
- A Flow Guard in an Activity diagram.
- A State Invariant in a Sequence diagram.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning Event Type' space='MED']

To add a hyperlink on a guard expression, State Invariant, and Change Event

1. Select one of the following:
  - guard expression on Transition in State Machine diagram.
  - guard expression on Flow in Activity diagram.
  - State Invariant in Sequence diagram.
  - Change Event expression in State Machine diagram.
2. Press Ctrl+Space or Ctrl+****Backspace. If you press Ctrl+K, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Edit Hyperlink dialog' space=''] opens and you can insert a hyperlink to an appropriate property. Makesure the selected property is correct.
3. Select the property from the list. The list provides all parameters, structural members of behavior, and structural members of the contextual classifier of behavior. The hyperlinked property is added according to the default setting of the **Update Mode** and **Text to display** list boxes. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Default settings of hyperlinks to model elements' space='']How to change the default settings of hyperlinks to model elements >> For example, in the figure below, the hyperlink to the *currentSpeed* Property is added on the guard expression. It is updated after renaming the *currentSpeed* Property to *speed*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The hyperlink to the currentSpeed Property is added on the guard expression. It is updated after renaming the currentSpeed Property to speed in the Model Browser.

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining hyperlinks' space='']

- Linking model elements to OSLC resources
- Managing hyperlinks
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='HTML editor' space='']

ac:macro-id="85a9d6c6-af1c-426c-8105-b9eeaf5c5e65"><ac:parameter ac:name="">element</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Adding hyperlink in text to element/symbol</h4><p>Two additional ways to add a hyperlink in text to an element/symbol include:</p><ul><li data-uuid="e72088b0-257d-47f7-b0c8-29d3215a27d3"><ac:link ac:anchor="drag"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[By dragging element on Note or Text box shape.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="bea76c4e-ed91-44f0-b5c0-4056711060bf"><ac:link ac:anchor="guards"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[By pressing Ctrl+Space on Transition, Flow Guards, State Invariant, or Change Event expressions.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p class="confluence-link"><strong> </strong></p><h4 class="confluence-link"><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd55f9d8-159b-4773-8126-7eba57c91c83"><ac:parameter ac:name="">drag</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>Adding hyperlink to element by dragging element on Note or Text box shape</h4><p>This method of adding hyperlinks in text is useful only when inserting a hyperlink to element on Note or Text box shapes.</p><p>To add a hyperlink to an element by dragging an element on a Note or Text box shape</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="b85d29f7-2f3e-44c7-8d17-79a2c42b80ac">In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, select one or more elements.</li><li data-uuid="81076152-8487-4715-ad15-c93c6ff8a5bf"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Drag the selected elements onto the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Note" /></ac:link> or the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Text Box" /></ac:link> shapes on the diagram pane. <br />The hyperlink to the element Specification window is created on the Note or Text Box. The text become underlined and blue.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cffdb39f-bfe9-491f-9648-f676f1521eec"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <span class="current"><strong>Update Mode</strong> and <strong>Text to display</strong> values are selected according default settings</span>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Default settings of hyperlinks to model elements" /><ac:link-body>How to change the default settings of <span class="current">hyperlinks to model elements</span> &gt;&gt;</ac:link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4 class="with-breadcrumbs"><strong><span class="current"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2179cfa6-bd59-4956-ad24-230dc82cb51c"><ac:parameter ac:name="">guards</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span></strong><span class="current">Adding hyperlinks to elements on Guard expressions, Change Event expressions, and State Invariant</span></h4><p>This method of adding hyperlinks in text is useful when inserting hyperlinks to properties on:</p><ul><li data-uuid="41245e7a-6474-4acb-8a9c-7cedc523d04d">A Transition Guard in a State Machine diagram.</li><li data-uuid="f53acf86-91f1-427c-8b31-ef8c47ca6e4b">A Flow Guard in an Activity diagram.</li><li data-uuid="edab1fd6-d80a-4294-b993-2c57f5292017">A State Invariant in a Sequence diagram.</li><li data-uuid="b8ab0613-a44b-45e2-b7f1-89a6c82143f9"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Assigning Event Type" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Change Event expression in a State Machine diagram.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To add a hyperlink on a guard expression, State Invariant, and Change Event</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="6dad912f-6c80-4265-add0-b4314c7e1e60">Select one of the following:<br /><ul><li>guard expression on Transition in State Machine diagram.</li><li>guard expression on Flow in Activity diagram.</li><li>State Invariant in Sequence diagram.</li><li>Change Event expression in State Machine diagram. </li></ul></li><li data-uuid="91d0e608-83af-426d-90d4-98261e297d7a"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Press Ctrl+Space or Ctrl+<strong> </strong>Backspace.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="57e731a0-9fbf-4ba6-94c2-467137bc7b40"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you press Ctrl+K, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Edit Hyperlink dialog" /></ac:link> opens and you can insert a hyperlink to an appropriate property. Make<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> sure the selected property is correct.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="0f444c60-8723-4aaa-a59d-8a6379757dbb"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select the property from the list.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e6f19df2-7086-4d4b-bd80-70b5a3f59e16"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The list provides all parameters, structural members of behavior, and structural members of the contextual classifier of behavior.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">The hyperlinked property is added according to the default setting of the <span class="current"><strong>Update Mode</strong> and <strong>Text to display</strong> list boxes</span>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Default settings of hyperlinks to model elements" /><ac:link-body>How to change the default settings of <span class="current">hyperlinks to model elements</span> &gt;&gt;</ac:link-body></ac:link></p><p class="auto-cursor-target">For example, in the figure below, the hyperlink to the <em>currentSpeed</em> Property is added on the guard expression. It is updated after renaming the <em>currentSpeed</em> Property to <em>speed</em>.</p></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="updated_hyperlink_on_guard_expression.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The hyperlink to the currentSpeed Property is added on the guard expression. It is updated after renaming the currentSpeed Property to speed in the Model Browser.</h6><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="054a0b80-eea1-4440-8572-ed2b18831aaa"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Defining hyperlinks" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><ul><li data-uuid="1243e9e8-854b-446f-8172-c01b89eb11c6"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/CDH/?contextKey=linking-model-elements-to-oslc-resources&amp;version=latest">Linking model elements to OSLC resources</a></li><li data-uuid="4035b4a7-2a01-4c08-a32d-af05c3ff8ca5"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/DEVG/?contextKey=managing-hyperlinks&amp;version=latest">Managing hyperlinks</a></li><li data-uuid="01ec0f38-3881-4adb-a35d-9718c78b2e3e"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="HTML editor" /></ac:link></li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968385 space=MT version=10 -->
## PAGE 05473: Hyperlinks on elements

- page_id: `243968385`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968385/Hyperlinks+on+elements
- version_number: 10
- version_date: `2025-09-11T19:49:37.952+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Manage Navigation > Defining hyperlinks
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Hyperlinked elements or diagrams allow you to quickly navigate through the model, to other resources, or between them. If an element or diagram refers to the , you can access that content by double clicking an element or an entire diagram. You can add all [types of hyperlinks](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Edit+Hyperlink+dialog#EditHyperlinkdialog-Hyperlinktypes) on elements and diagrams.

##### Adding a hyperlink on an element or entire diagram

To add a hyperlink on an element or entire diagram

1. Select the element or diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Click the smart manipulator [IMAGE alt='' src='']**Go To** > **Manage Navigation**.
  - From the shortcut menu, choose **Go To** > **Manage Navigation**.
3. The **Specification** window opens. Choose the **Navigation/Hyperlinks** property group, and click the **Add** button.
4. In the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Edit Hyperlink dialog' space='']**Edit Hyperlink** dialog, select a tab according to the [hyperlink type](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Edit+Hyperlink+dialog#EditHyperlinkdialog-Hyperlinktypes) you want to insert.
5. Specify the boxes and options according to the selected tab. The meaning of boxes and options of each tab is described in the following pages: 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Address tab' space='']**Address** tab 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='File tab' space='']**File** tab 
 - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element Symbol tab' space='']**Element/Symbol** tab 
 - [**OSLC Link** tab](https://docs.nomagic.com/CDH/?contextKey=linking-model-elements-to-oslc-resources&version=latest)The **Text to display** box does not exist in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Edit Hyperlink dialog' space='']**Edit Hyperlink** dialog.
6. Select the **Active** check box to activate the hyperlink. Only one hyperlink can be active. By default the last added hyperlink is active. You can also:
  - Click the **Open** button, to open the content of the hyperlink, e.g., a webpage or another external resource, a file on your filesystem, or an element/symbol.
  - Click the **Edit** button, to edit an existing hyperlink in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Edit Hyperlink dialog' space='']**Edit Hyperlink** dialog.
  - Click the **Add** button, to add a new hyperlink in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Edit Hyperlink dialog' space='']**Edit Hyperlink** dialog.
  - Click the **Remove** button, to delete the hyperlink. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Click **OK**. 
The hyperlink is added on the selected element or diagram. In the Model Browser, you will see the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Hyperlinks/Go To icon on the element. On the diagram pane, on the symbol, you will see the icon representing the subject the hyperlink is added to. You can instantly open a hyperlink by double clicking an element or diagram.

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining hyperlinks' space='']

- Linking model elements to OSLC resources
- Managing hyperlinks
