
<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249005586 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05508: Importing ReqIF files from the command-line interface

- page_id: `249005586`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249005586/Importing+ReqIF+files+from+the+command-line+interface
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-17T23:11:20.952+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Requirements management > Importing requirements > Importing ReqIF files
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Importing from the command-line interface is useful when you want to import files automatically, e.g., once a day at midnight.

To import a ReqIF file from the command-line interface

1. Open the Command Prompt window
2. Go to the <model editor installationdirectory>/plugins/com.nomagic.requirements directory containing the *reqifimport.exe* file.
3. At the command prompt, type the following command and specify the values of the required arguments: reqifimport project_descriptor=<project URL> reqif_file=<path to ReqIF file> owner=<element hyperlink or qualified name> Where:
  - **project_descriptor** is the URL of a project. How to get a project descriptor?Start your modeling tool and open the required project.In the main menu, go to **File** > **Project Properties** and select the **General** tab.Find the project descriptor specified in the **Project Descriptor** box at the bottom of the **Project Properties** dialog and copy it to the clipboard.
  - **reqif_file** is the path to the ReqIF file you want to import. If there is a space in a path, surround the argument and its value with escaped quote(\").
  - **owner** is the hyperlink or qualified name of the owner element. If newly imported elements are not contained by the Specification element, they are placed under the specified owner. Note that the **owner** argument is optional. How to get the hyperlink of an element?Start your modeling tool and open the required project.In the Containment tree, right-click an element and select **Copy Element Hyperlink**.The hyperlink of the element is now copied to the clipboard.
4. Press **Enter**.

Now after opening the project you have imported the ReqIF file into, the imported data is already present in the Containment tree of the project.

##### Using a properties file

To make importing from the command-line interface quicker you can specify arguments in a properties file, which can be easily edited or reused.

To import a ReqIF file from the command-line interface using a properties file

1. Create a properties file and specify the values of the following arguments: See the example of a properties file below. text1trueProject Properties > General.
#
reqif_file=C:/Users/johnsmith/Documents/project_requirements.reqif
#
# Specify the path to the ReqIF file.
# NOTE: If there is a space in a path, surround the argument and its value with escaped quote (\").
#
owner=mdel://eee_1045467100313_135436_1
#
# Specify the hyperlink or qualified name of the owner element. 
# To get the hyperlink of an element, open a project, right-click the element in the Containment tree and select "Copy Element Hyperlink".
# NOTE: This argument is optional.
#]]> When creating a properties file on Windows OS, use only / or \\ as a path separator, otherwise the command will fail.
  - project_descriptor
  - reqif_file
  - owner
2. Open the Command Prompt window.
3. Go to the <model editor installationdirectory>/plugins/com.nomagic.requirements directory containing the *reqifimport.exe* file.
4. At the command prompt, type the following command and specify the value of the **properties_file** argument. reqifimport properties_file=<path to properties file> Where **properties_file** is the path to the properties file to be used.
5. Press **Enter**.

Now after opening the project you have imported the ReqIF file into, the imported data is already present in the Containment tree of the project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
# NOTE: This argument is optional.
#]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a747b836-052e-4bff-a358-e5edaed77878"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>When creating a properties file on Windows OS, use only / or \\ as a path separator, otherwise the command will fail.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Open the Command Prompt window.</li><li>Go to the <em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">&lt;model editor installa</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">tion </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">directory&gt;/plugins/com.nomagic.requirements</span></em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> directory containing the <em>reqifimport.exe</em> file.</span></li><li><p>At the command prompt, type the following command and specify the value of the <strong>properties_file</strong> argument.</p><pre>reqifimport properties_file=&lt;path to properties file&gt;</pre><p>Where <strong>properties_file</strong> is the path to the properties file to be used.<br /><br /></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);line-height: 1.42857;">Press <strong>Enter</strong>.</span></span></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);line-height: 1.42857;">Now after opening the project you have imported the ReqIF file into, the imported data is already present in the Containment tree of the project.<br /></span></span></span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965835 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05510: Improving performance

- page_id: `243965835`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965835/Improving+performance
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:47.195+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Getting started
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1427324068

1427324070

1427324069

INLINE

When working with very large models or a number of diagrams at a time, the modeling tool performance may decrease. To increase efficiency, we suggest the following solutions:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Memory allocation' space=''] (java heap size). If you want to make sure you have a sufficient amount of memory allocated to your modeling tool, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Memory monitor' space=''] to track memory usage.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Active validation period' space=''] and/or do not keep unused diagrams open.
- Split a project into several separate project files, and load only the files that you work with. This may help only if your project contains several parts with minimal dependencies between them. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project partitioning' space=''].
- Use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Memory monitor' space=''] to free unused memory.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Background Task Manager' space=''] that run too long.
- Set the limit of open diagrams in the Environment Options dialog ( Options > Environment > General > General > Diagram Tabs Limit ).

1427324067

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Getting started' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing and Selecting Perspective' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Configuration files' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Disabling inbound network activity' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support_obsolete' space='NMDOC']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Maps to a UML Component with the stereotype XSDinclude. This component must be added into an xsd file component.

- annotation maps to the UML Component documentation
- schemaLocation maps to the UML Component name.

#### PANEL: include XML representation summary

include XML representation summary

```text
<include
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
schemaLocation = anyURI
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>
```

```text
Content: (annotation?)
```

```text
</include>
```

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### include UML model example.

#### PANEL: include XML code sample

include XML code sample

```text
<xs:schema xmlns:nm = "http://nomagic.com" xmlns:xs = "http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" targetNamespace = "http://nomagic.com">	<xs:include schemaLocation = "http://nomagic.com/schema.xsd" /></xs:schema>
```

## PAGE 05512: Including external images

- page_id: `243970837`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243970837/Including+external+images
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:59.957+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Image tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050285233

2050285245

2050285231

**On this page**

43

2050285234

You can include images from an external source in a report. The supported image types are *BMP*, *JPG*, *WBMP*, *GIF*, and *PNG*.

###### **$image.include(location)**

Include an external image from a local file or *URL* in a report.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | location | java.lang.String | An image’s location. The location format can be either a URL or a local file. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image included in a report |

For example:

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="adcb2062-dfa2-4b21-9dcd-3e6517f349c8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050285233</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2aaa8a70-e325-4c59-8f8d-45f736106588"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050285245</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="897d6481-21c5-4b5d-8590-c2a4a1b7ae7c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050285231</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab4f9a35-a6c1-4036-9b04-feb095c74624"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd7876af-2c40-481d-9c44-715984bb4606"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050285234</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can include images from an external source in a report. The supported image types are <em>BMP</em>, <em>JPG</em>, <em>WBMP</em>, <em>GIF</em>, and <em>PNG</em>.</p><h4><strong>$image.include(location)</strong></h4><p>Include an external image from a local file or <em>URL</em> in a report.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s)</td><td>location</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An image’s location. <br />The location format <br />can be either a URL <br />or a local file.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>An image included <br />in a report</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd86fd2e-acc9-4364-82e6-34794a984140"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$image.include("C:\Users\username\images\logo.gif")
$image.include("http://www.magicdraw.com/images/product_boxes/MD.gif")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249566700 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05513: Inclusive Gateway

- page_id: `249566700`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249566700/Inclusive+Gateway
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:22:12.348+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > BPMN Process Diagram > Gateways
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1485738387

1485738387

1485738387

**Description**

A diverging Inclusive Gateway (Inclusive Decision) is used to create not only alternative but also parallel paths within a process flow. Unlike an Exclusive Gateway, it evaluates all condition expressions. The true evaluation of one condition expression does not exclude the evaluation of the other condition expressions. All of the sequence flows with true evaluation will be traversed by a token.

Since each path is considered to be independent, all combinations of the paths may be taken, from zero to all. However, it should be designed in such a way that at least one path is taken.

A converging Inclusive Gateway is used to merge a combination of alternative and parallel paths. A control flow token arriving at an Inclusive Gateway may be synchronized with some other tokens that arrive later at this Gateway.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Diverging Inclusive Gateway

1485738386

**Related elements**

- Gateways
- Sequence Flow

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedure**

- Creating and Using a Sequence Flow

Unlike an Exclusive Gateway, it evaluates all condition expressions. The true evaluation of one condition expression does not exclude the evaluation of the other condition expressions. All of the sequence flows with true evaluation will be traversed by a token.</p><p>Since each path is considered to be independent, all combinations of the paths may be taken, from zero to all. However, it should be designed in such a way that at least one path is taken.</p><p>A converging Inclusive Gateway is used to merge a combination of alternative and parallel paths. A control flow token arriving at an Inclusive Gateway may be synchronized with some other tokens that arrive later at this Gateway.</p><p><strong>Notation</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="gateway_inclusive.png" /></ac:image></p><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Diverging_inclusive_gateway.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Diverging Inclusive Gateway</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca9df4a0-8117-467a-9230-51a9a9d2126f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1485738386</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Gateways">Gateways</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Sequence+Flow">Sequence Flow</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/BPMN+Process+Diagram">BPMN Process Diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/BPMN+Collaboration+Diagram">BPMN Collaboration Diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/BPMN+Choreography+Diagram">BPMN Choreography Diagram</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedure</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Creating+and+Using+a+Sequence+Flow">Creating and Using a Sequence Flow</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968234 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05514: Incompatible properties and related elements

- page_id: `243968234`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968234/Incompatible+properties+and+related+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:53.986+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Refactoring > Converting Elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After the conversion, a converted element owns the same compatible specification properties and is connected with the same related elements as the original element was connected. The incompatible properties of the original element are lost, that is, are deleted from the project.

Incompatible means that the converted element can NOT own particular properties or can NOT be associated with particular related elements of the original element.

For example, let’s convert the component to package. See an example in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As you see, the component has the assigned port. According to UML guidelines, a package cannot own ports. So, in this case, ports are incompatible with the packages. After the conversion, the package, that is, a converted element, has no assigned port. The port is deleted from the project.

To prevent from losing model data, you always get a message with a warning that incompatible properties will be lost after the element conversion.

##### Changes after conversion

###### Converting a property to a port

If the property visibility is *private*, *protected,* or *package* , then after the conversion visibility of the created port will be set to *public*.

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

The incompatible properties of the original element are lost, that is, are deleted from the project.</p><p>Incompatible means that the converted element can NOT own particular properties or can NOT be associated with particular related elements of the original element.</p><p>For example, let’s convert the component to package. See an example in the following figure.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Example of conversion when original and converted elements has incompatible property (Port)" ac:title="Example of conversion when original and converted elements has incompatible property (Port)"><ri:attachment ri:filename="converting_elements_incompatible_elements.png" /></ac:image></p><p>As you see, the component has the assigned port. According to UML guidelines, a package cannot own ports. So, in this case, ports are incompatible with the packages. After the conversion, the package, that is, a converted element, has no assigned port. The port is deleted from the project.</p><p>To prevent from losing model data, you always get a message with a warning that incompatible properties will be lost after the element conversion.</p><h3>Changes after conversion</h3><h4>Converting a property to a port</h4><p>If the property visibility is <em>private</em>, <em>protected,</em> or <em>package</em> , then after the conversion visibility of the created port will be set to <em>public</em>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67b51616-1257-45d8-b179-df6555185bfc"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On this page</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2fc359cb-dee4-4291-9163-fcd677e10dd5" /> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966095 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05515: Indexing

- page_id: `243966095`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966095/Indexing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:52.950+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Searching
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Model elements representation text indexing allows users to find elements in the model more quickly. It starts automatically when a Teamwork Cloud project is opened for the first time; you can track the progress in the status bar (see the image below). The index is automatically updated when the model changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The indexing progress shown in the status bar.

Please note that indexing may take some time depending on the project size. The more elements the project contains, the longer it takes to index the model elements representation text.

If representation text building is in progress, searches performed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space=''] dialog may be slower. In this case, a warning appears next to the search progress bar (see an image below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If a search does not return expected results, we recommend that you rebuild the model elements representation text index (see below).

The**Rebuild Text Index** action is available in the Expert mode only.

instructionsTo rebuild the text index

- In the main menu , click Tools > Rebuild Text Index . [ATTACHMENT filename='rebuild_text_index_dialog.png']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966137 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05516: Indexing of unloaded projects

- page_id: `243966137`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966137/Indexing+of+unloaded+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:53.590+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Decomposing model
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Proxy

628699399

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Proxy

628699401

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Proxy

628699400

If you have a large project and, due to performance issues, some used projects are not loaded, you may use indexing to work with the project smoothly.Indexing can be considered as an intermediate form of work, falling between working with a fully loaded used project and working with a not-loaded used project.

#### INFO: Proxy

Proxy

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Maintaining decomposed model integrity' space='']true

When a used project is unloaded/not loaded in the project, only proxies of necessary elements are shown in place of the used project. Using indexing allows you to show more proxy elements from the unloaded project. These proxies can be used as normal model elements in the project using them without loading the used project. Keeping the used project not loaded saves a considerable amount of computer resources. If you ever need full information from that used project, you can load it at any time.

A typical case where this functionality is useful is outlined below:

A large software library is used in a project. Only certain classes are important for the main project: some library classes are set as types of properties in the model classes, some model classes inherit from the library classes, etc. In this case, the sub-elements of the library classes (their properties and methods) are not important, and we can index classes in the library and keep it not loaded in the main project. Indexing will still show all the classes in the main project from the library.

To specify elements for indexing

1. Open the used project (that is, not loaded) separately from the using project.
2. From the Options menu, select Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
3. Select the Indexing property group. [ATTACHMENT filename='project_options_indexing.png']
4. Select the **Common Indexing** option. This enables indexing of the used project and determines what information will be indexed.
5. When common indexing is chosen, classifiers and their inheritance relationships will be indexed. If you want more elements to be indexed, select the **Custom Indexing** option and fine-tune which element types (properties, methods, etc) should be indexed. The more elements you select, the more elements will be accessible in the project using them as proxies. However, your gains in performance from the used project unloading will also diminish. Thus, a balance is needed when customizing the index. It is usually better to use the common indexing variant. Click **OK**.
6. In the project using the indexed project, go to the options of the used indexed project (from the main menu, select **Options** > **Project Usages**) and select the **Use Index** check box.

628699398

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Maintaining decomposed model integrity' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b67429c1-fe33-441f-95d4-891a75370dfe"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628699399</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5323c54-ea67-42f4-b33b-56df6d2842e0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628699401</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a7a20e1-4dc1-4bd9-bb11-8b409706345d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628699400</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="291b13b2-a9df-4d8c-9830-9a083f16b245">If you have a large project and, due to performance issues, some used projects are not loaded, you may use indexing to work with the project smoothly. </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="291b13b2-a9df-4d8c-9830-9a083f16b245">Indexing</ac:inline-comment-marker> can be considered as an intermediate form of work, falling between working with a fully loaded used project and working with a not-loaded used project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1c1ae84-92a9-410a-9e4a-0cda821f843b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Proxy</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt-include" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e66c221-e641-4588-b01e-0fca83433f19"><ac:parameter ac:name=""><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Maintaining decomposed model integrity" /></ac:link></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="nopanel">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>When a used project is unloaded/not loaded in the project, only proxies of <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c07af6fa-3fd0-4554-a664-3e5b51394736">necessary element</ac:inline-comment-marker>s are shown in place of the used project. Using indexing allows you to show more proxy elements from the unloaded project. These proxies can be used as normal model elements in the project using them without loading the used project. Keeping the used project not loaded saves a considerable amount of computer resources.  If you ever need full information from that used project, you can load it at any time.</p><p>A typical case where this functionality is useful is outlined below:</p><p>A large software library is used in a project. Only certain classes are important for the main project: some library classes are set as types of properties in the model classes, some model classes inherit from the library classes, etc. In this case, the sub-elements of the library classes (their properties and methods) are not important, and we can index classes in the library and keep it not loaded in the main project. Indexing will still show all the classes in the main project from the library.</p><p><br /></p><p>To specify elements for <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="108124cd-4ada-4792-92e5-e3120682817a">indexing</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="77374505-fd83-4671-b796-37de755e139c">Open the used project (that is, not loaded) separately from the using project.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select the <strong>Indexing</strong> property group.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_options_indexing.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p>Select the <strong>Common Indexing</strong> option. This enables indexing of the used project and determines what information will be indexed.</p></li><li><p>When common indexing is chosen, classifiers and their inheritance relationships will be indexed. If you want more elements to be indexed, select the <strong>Custom Indexing</strong> option and fine-tune which element types (properties, methods, etc) should be indexed. The more elements you select, the more elements will be accessible in the project using them as proxies. However, your gains in performance from the used project unloading will also diminish. Thus, a balance is needed when customizing the index. It is usually better to use the common indexing variant.</p><p>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p></li><li><p>In the project using the indexed project, go to the options of the used indexed project (from the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>) and select the <strong>Use Index</strong> check box. </p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d3bdc33-2e2d-4238-8f19-7a0e4fd3b85c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628699398</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Maintaining decomposed model integrity" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 05517: Influence Concepts Relationship

- page_id: `249566887`

1487644646

1487644646

The following table provides the relationship that exists between element types and the meaning.

| Relationship | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Is Source Of | This link connects to an Influencer ( or ).Meaning that the Influencing Organization is the source of Influencer. An Influencer may have multiple sources, ornone. |  |

1487644644

**Related elements**

**Related diagram**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="66e16dd4-09bf-445a-bbef-e965d72dacd3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1487644645</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0a2d9bf8-d631-4829-b030-3e8644b1afda"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1487644646</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ef72f35d-871c-49f4-b169-d89ae8ae89e8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1487644646</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The following table provides the relationship that exists between element types and the meaning.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Relationship </th><th>Description </th><th><p>Example</p></th></tr><tr><td>Is Source Of </td><td><p>This link connects <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Influencing Organization" /></ac:link> to an Influencer (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="External Influencer" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Internal Influencer" /></ac:link>).<br />Meaning that the Influencing Organization is the source of Influencer. An Influencer may have multiple sources, or<br />none.</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="is_source_of.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9d8af5d1-b8e2-4f1b-97d5-d1fb7e97504d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1487644644</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Influencer+Concepts">Influencer Concepts</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagram</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Business+Motivation+Diagram">Business Motivation Diagram</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 05519: Influencing Organization

- page_id: `249566884`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249566884/Influencing+Organization
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:22:20.901+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Supportive Diagrams Concepts > Business Motivation Diagram > Influencer Concepts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1487609551

1487609552

1487609552

**Description**

An Influencing Organization is an organization that is external to the enterprise modeled in a given enterprise BMM, and that influences that enterprise.

An Influencing Organization is the source of Influencer. The Influencer may have multiple sources, or none.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1487609550

**Related elements**

- Influencer Concepts
- Influence Concepts Relationship

**Related diagram**

- Business Motivation Diagram

1488087165

1488087166

1488087166

**Description**

An Information System is a type of hardware or a software, e. g., international sales system, ticket reservation system, etc. Information system can be used by Process or Process Activity.

**Notation**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965745 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05521: Inheritance tab

- page_id: `243965745`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965745/Inheritance+tab
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-25T10:57:43.123+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Getting started > Understanding the user interface > Model Browser
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1425598369

1425598371

1425598370

INLINE

The Inheritance tab represents classifiers (e.g., packages, data types, and stereotypes) hierarchy within your project. Inheritance according to the UML Specification is shown using the generalization relationship.

If a model element has no generalization relationship, it will not be represented in the Inheritance tab.

To open a new Inheritance tree

1. Open the Containment , Structure , or Inheritance tab.
2. In the open tab toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='open_new_tab_button.png'] and then select Open in New Inheritance Tree .

You can change some Inheritance tab properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']**Environment Options** dialog in the **Browser** options group.

The Inheritance tab consists of the following two components:

- Inheritance tab toolbar
- Inheritance tree

##### Inheritance tab toolbar

The Inheritance tab toolbar contains the following buttons

| Button | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Collapse All | Collapses all currently expanded elements in the Containment tree. |
|  | Collapse Selected Recursively | Collapses only selected node and all subnodes in that node. |
|  | Open in New Tree | Opens the contents of the selected element in a new tab of the Model Browser. The selected element becomes the root node of the new tree.You can choose to open elements in:New Containment TreeNew Structure TreeNew Inheritance Tree |
|  | Favorites | Opens the Favorites menu for choosing to do one of the following:Add a selected element to favoritesOpen the dialog for managing favorites Navigate to a desired favorite in the Containment tree |
|  | Quick Find | Opens the Quick Find dialog. |
|  | Options | Click to open the Options menu and then select:Invert Tree - inverts the tree hierarchy - shows the childelement at the top of the hierarchy.Show Applied Stereotypes - shows or hides the applied stereotypes next to the element name in the Containment tree. |

##### Working with model elements in the Inheritance tree

You can work with elements in the Inheritance tree using the commands available from the selected element's shortcut menu. The shortcut menu contains the following commands:

| Command | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Select in Containment Tree | Opens the Containment tab and selects the current element in the Containment tree. |
| Select in Structure Tree | Opens the Structure tab and the structured classifier is selected.This command is available for structured classifiers (SysML v1). |

1425598368

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565413 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05522: Inheritance, Aggregation, Composition, and Redefinition of Exchanges

- page_id: `249565413`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565413/Inheritance+Aggregation+Composition+and+Redefinition+of+Exchanges
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:48.036+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures > Working with exchanges
- labels: ['inheritance-of-exchanges', 'aggregation-of-exchanges', 'composition-of-exchanges', 'redefinition-of-exchanges', 'aggregated-information', 'display-all-operational-exchanges', 'hide-inherited-exchanges', 'see-inherited-exchanges', 'redefine-operational-exchange', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational/Resource/Service Exchanges can be inherited, aggregated, or composed. You can redefine the inherited flows.

You can see the inherited, aggregated, and composed exchanges in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space=''] dialog
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space=''] dialog
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space=''] dialog
- Operational/Resources/Services/Personnel/Security (Operational and Resource) Role-based Connectivity tables.

The simplified scenarios illustrating the Aggregation and Inherited Exchanges are described in the following cases:

###### Case 1. Aggregated Exchanges

Let's say, we have Operational Performers *Helicopter*and *Signal Receiver* related to the Operational Exchange *Reported Condition*. Then we draw an association between *Medical Assistance* and *Signal Receiver*. In this case, the drawn association can realize the *Reported Condition* exchange, since the Operational Performer *Medical Assistance* aggregates the Operational Performer *Helicopter*.

If Composition is used instead of Aggregation to connect Medical Assistance and Helicopter, the Exchanges will be collected the same way as in the Aggregation case.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To hide aggregated exchanges in dialogs

1. Click Options > Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog, select General > UAF .
3. Set the Respect UAF Information Flows on Aggregation property value to false .

- The exchange is not created for the drawn association, it is only realized.
- The realization of the exchange will be removed from the association when Aggregation or Exchange is removed.

All the aggregated exchanges can also be seen in the role-based connectivity tables. The aggregated exchange rows are displayed in gray.

To see the aggregated exchanges

1. On the table toolbar, select Show Rows.
2. In the open menu, click the Aggregated Operational Exchange or Resource **Exchange** command.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Case 2. Inherited Exchanges

Let's say, we have an Operational Performers *Search**Node* and *Place Of Safety* related to the Operational Exchange *Warning Order*. The Operational Performer *Aircraft* specifies the Operational Performer *Search**Node*. Then we draw an association between *Aircraft* and *Place O**f**Safety*. In this case, the drawn association inherits the Operational Exchange information that can be realized and redefined.

Let's realize the Operational Exchange. We can do this in two ways:

- By clicking [ATTACHMENT filename='display_all_operational_exchanges.png'] in the association smart manipulator toolbar.
- By selecting the check box in the Realize column of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational****Exchange****Manager** dialog .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To hide inherited exchanges in dialogs

1. Click Options > Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog, select General > UAF .
3. Set the Respect UAF Information Flows on Generalization property value to false .

- The exchange is not created for the drawn association, it is only realized.
- The realization of the exchange will be removed from the association when Generalization or Exchange is removed.

All the inherited exchanges can also be seen in the role-based connectivity tables. The inherited exchange rows are displayed in gray.

To see the inherited exchanges

1. On the table toolbar, select Show Rows.
2. In the open menu, click the Inherited Operational Exchange or Resource **Exchange** command.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

When we have the inherited Operational Exchange, we can redefine it. This means, that the inherited Operational Exchange will be copied together with the information such as Actual Measurements, Conveyed Items, Documentation. The new Operational Exchange will be created in the model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To redefine the Operational Exchange

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational****Exchange****Manager** dialog , select the inherited Operational Exchange and click the Redefine button. The new Operational Exchange is created.

All the information that comes from the inherited exchange when redefining it can be modified in the model.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Strategic Exchange wizard' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Operational/Resource/Service Exchanges can be inherited, aggregated, or composed. You can redefine the inherited flows.</p><p>You can see the inherited, aggregated, and composed exchanges in:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Exchange Manager]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> dialog</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resource Exchange Manager]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> dialog</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Service Exchange Manager]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> dialog</li><li>Operational/Resources/Services/Personnel/Security (Operational and Resource) Role-based Connectivity tables.<span> </span></li></ul><p><span>The simplified scenarios illustrating the Aggregation and Inherited Exchanges are described in the following cases:</span></p><p><span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dba0c654-686a-4b34-b644-0ce46be559ac" /></span></p><h4><span>Case 1. Aggregated Exchanges</span></h4><p><span>Let's say, we have Operational Performers <em>Helicopter </em>and <em>Signal Receiver</em> related to the Operational Exchange <em>Reported Condition</em>. Then we draw an association between <em>Medical Assistance</em> and <em>Signal Receiver</em>. In this case, the drawn association can realize the <em>Reported Condition</em> exchange, since the Operational Performer <em>Medical Assistance</em> aggregates the Operational Performer <em>Helicopter</em>.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b773f29c-06f7-438b-abbb-e7df6ba917c0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If Composition is used instead of Aggregation to connect Medical Assistance and Helicopter, the Exchanges will be collected the same way as in the Aggregation case.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span><ac:image ac:title="Aggregation of information flow" ac:alt="Aggregation of information flow"><ri:attachment ri:filename="aggregated_information.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cc237fa9-641c-4802-a43e-fcc177c0b73f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To hide aggregated exchanges in dialogs</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Options </strong>&gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the<strong> Project Options</strong> dialog, select <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>UAF</strong>.</li><li>Set the <strong>Respect</strong><strong> UAF </strong><strong>Information</strong><strong> Flows </strong><strong>on</strong><strong> Aggregation</strong> property value to <em>false</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7614ef55-b73c-44c6-ba9a-bda57482510b"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The exchange is not created for the drawn association, it is only realized.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The realization of the exchange will be removed from the association when Aggregation or Exchange is removed.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>All the aggregated exchanges can also be seen in the role-based connectivity tables. The aggregated exchange rows are displayed in gray. </p><p><br /></p><p>To see the aggregated exchanges</p><hr /><ol><li>On the table toolbar, select<strong> Show</strong><strong> Rows.</strong></li><li>In the open menu, click the <strong>Aggregated Operational</strong><strong> Exchange</strong> or <strong>Resource</strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong> Exchange </strong></span>command.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="see_aggregated.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Case 2. Inherited Exchanges</h4><p>Let's say, we have an Operational Performers <em>Search </em><em>Node</em> and <em>Place Of Safety</em> related to the Operational Exchange <em>Warning Order</em>. The Operational Performer <em>Aircraft</em> specifies the Operational Performer <em>Search</em><em> Node</em>. Then we draw an association between <em>Aircraft</em> and <em>Place O</em><em>f</em><em> Safety</em>. In this case, the drawn association inherits the Operational Exchange information that can be realized and redefined.</p><p>Let's realize the Operational Exchange. We can do this in two ways:</p><ul><li>By clicking <ac:image ac:title="Display All Operational Exchanges" ac:alt="Display All Operational Exchanges"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_all_operational_exchanges.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the association smart manipulator toolbar.</li><li>By selecting the check box in the Realize column of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational</strong><strong> Exchange</strong><strong> Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="inheritance_in_operational_exchange_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e872cb39-c48c-491e-9d0e-92fee41834fa"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To hide inherited exchanges in dialogs</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Options </strong>&gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the<strong> Project Options</strong> dialog, select <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>UAF</strong>.</li><li>Set the <strong>Respect</strong><strong> UAF </strong><strong>Information</strong><strong> Flows </strong><strong>on</strong><strong> Generalization</strong> property value to <em>false</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06847455-5bf0-4a08-bbb6-30cd3273d138"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The exchange is not created for the drawn association, it is only realized.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The realization of the exchange will be removed from the association when Generalization or Exchange is removed.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />All the inherited exchanges can also be seen in the role-based connectivity tables. The inherited exchange rows are displayed in gray. </p><p><br /></p><p>To see the inherited exchanges</p><hr /><ol><li>On the table toolbar, select<strong> Show</strong><strong> Rows.</strong></li><li>In the open menu, click the <strong>Inherited</strong><strong> Operational</strong><strong> Exchange</strong> or <strong>Resource</strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong> Exchange </strong></span>command.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="inheritance_role_based.png" /></ac:image></p><p>When we have the inherited Operational Exchange, we can redefine it. This means, that the inherited Operational Exchange will be copied together with the information such as Actual Measurements, Conveyed Items, Documentation. The new Operational Exchange will be created in the model.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="The redefinition of Operational Exchange Warning Order" ac:alt="The redefinition of Operational Exchange Warning Order"><ri:attachment ri:filename="redefinition_of_inheritance.png" /></ac:image><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br /></p><p>To redefine the Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"> </span>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational</strong><strong> Exchange</strong><strong> Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, select the inherited Operational Exchange and click the <strong>Redefine</strong> button. The new Operational Exchange is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d8eb23ae-ce99-4912-b51e-a121df164715"><ac:rich-text-body><p>All the information that comes from the inherited exchange when redefining it can be modified in the model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with exchanges" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Service Exchange wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Strategic Exchange wizard" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561394 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 05523: Initial Values

- page_id: `249561394`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561394/Initial+Values
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:13:04.740+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling structure with Blocks
- labels: ['initial-values', 'context-specific-values', 'default-values', 'mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1345490194

1345490196

1345490195

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space=''] with a label of *initial values* is used to show values of properties belonging to a containing block. These values override any default values that may have been previously specified on these properties on their originally defining block. Initial value compartments may be specified within nested properties, which then apply only in the particular usage context defined by the outermost containing block.

The purpose of initial value compartments is to show various values as a result of a reconfigured selected context. Initial value compartments allows to:

- Create different configurations for the same structure and display them directly in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''] .
- Have different values for the same part in different contexts.
- Assign a different initial value to an inherited property.

The *initial value*s compartment can be shown on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] shape compartment and display the values of the properties reconfigured in a selected context.

The procedures below describes how to work with initial values and its compartments:

43

##### Defining initial values

You can define context-specific initial values by overriding any default values that was previously specified on Value Properties on their originally defining Block. Before defining a context-specific initial value, the followingprerequisites must be met:

- The Value Property must be created for a Block which is a type of Part Property.
- The Type and Default Value are defined for the Value Property in its Specification window.

To defineinitial value

1. Right-click the Part Property shape with default value defined.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Tools > **Define Context-Specific Initial Value**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. From the **Select Property** dialog, select Value Property which you want to redefine. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] After selecting the Value Property, the structure of instances is created automatically in the Model Browser.
4. Type the value directly on the Part Property shape. If you can not specify the new value, check if the context of that Part Property is correct. ]]>****
5. Press Enter. The slot with newly defined value is created for the Instance Specification. The default value is not changed. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Displaying/hiding *initial values* compartments

You can display/hide initial values on the Part Property Compartments. Read more about [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML v1 specific compartments' space=''].

To show/hide Initial Values on Part Property compartment, do one of the following

- Select the Part Property shape, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space=''] , and select Initial Values. [ATTACHMENT filename='initial_value_compartments.png']
- Right-click the Part Property shape and from the shortcut menu select Symbol Properties. In the Symbol Properties dialog, select the Expert or All mode, and set the Suppress Context Specific Values property value to true to show initial values, or false - to hide them.

##### contextSelecting the context of initial values

Initial values which are shown in the Part Property compartments depends from the selected context. For example, if the selected context is A then the compartment show values of A.

To select a context of initial values

1. Right-click the Part Property shape and from the shortcut menu select Symbol Properties.
2. In the Symbol Properties dialog, select the All mode.
3. Select the Context of Context-Specific Values property value box, and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_value_button_in_specification_window.png'] .
4. From the Select Element dialog, choose the element which will be the context.
5. Click OK .
6. Click Close . The context for initial values is specified.

##### Displaying/hiding element type in the *initial values* compartments

You can display or hide the elements types in the *initial values* compartments of a part using either the Symbol(s) Properties dialog or the part shortcut menu.

To display/hide element type in the *initial values* compartments

1. Right-click the Part Property shape and select the Symbol Properties command.
2. In the Symbol Properties dialog, select the Expert or All mode.
3. Select the Show Slot Type property value from drop-down list:

- 
  - None - to hide types.
  - Name - to display names of the element types.
  - Qualified Name - to display qualified names of the element types.

1345490192

**Related pages**

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Phone Camera** sample model. To open this sample download [ATTACHMENT filename='PhoneCamerasInitialValues.mdzip'].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967289 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05524: Inserting a shape on the path

- page_id: `243967289`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967289/Inserting+a+shape+on+the+path
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:27.413+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Working with paths and relationships
- labels: ['split-transition', 'insert-shape-on-path', 'split-path', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This functionality is available in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='']StateMachine and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space=''] diagrams.

In the State and Activity diagrams, you can split a path into two paths by dropping a shape on it. This is valid for Transition/ Control Flow/ Object Flow relationships and allows you to connect the element shape with the elements connected via the path. 
 
To insert a new shape by splitting the path on the diagram pane

1. Select the shape you want to insert or create a new one.
2. Drag the shape on the path (the path is highlighted in blue) and drop it.
3. In the **Split Transition** dialog, click the appropriate button according to where you want to insert the shape on the path:

- **Before Transition**button. The shape is inserted before the path. It means that a new path is created, then the dropped shape is drawn and then the existing path is drawn. For example, *Password read* transition is drawn from *Read Name* State to*Verification* State. If you want to insert the*Read password* State before the *Password read* Transition, drop the *Read password* State on the Transition, and in the **Split Transition** dialog, choose the **Before Transition** button.[IMAGE alt='' src=''][IMAGE alt='' src='']
- **After Transition**button. The shape is inserted before the path. It means the existing path is created, the dropped element symbol is drawn, and then a new path is drawn. For example, *Name**read* Transition is drawn from *Read Name* State to *Verification* State. If you want to insert the *Read**password* State after the *Name read* Transition, drop the *Read password* State on the Transition, and in the **Split Transition** dialog, choose the **After Transition** button.[IMAGE alt='' src=''][IMAGE alt='' src='']
- **Do Not Insert**button. Action is canceled and the dialog is closed.

In the **Split Transition** dialog, select the **Remember my choice** check box and the next time the shape will be inserted before or after the path, depending on your selection.

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be0eba18-1df8-4cbc-aa4e-4458ff1a6bfb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This functionality is available in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">State </span>Machine</ac:link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activity]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In the State and Activity diagrams, you can split a path into two paths by dropping a shape on it. This is valid for Transition/ Control Flow/ Object Flow relationships and allows you to connect the element shape with the elements connected via the path. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To insert a new shape by splitting the path on the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select the shape you want to insert or create a new one.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Drag the shape on the path (the path is highlighted in blue) and drop it.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> In the <strong>Split Transition</strong> dialog, click the appropriate button according to where you want to insert the shape on the path:</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></li></ol><ul><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Before Transition </strong>button. The shape is inserted before the path. It means that a new path is created, then the dropped shape is drawn and then the existing path is drawn.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b22b0b0c-fd35-4399-84a2-38e5a213de0b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">For example, <em>Password read</em> transition is drawn from <em>Read Name</em> State to<em> Verification</em> State. If you want to insert the<em> Read password</em> State before the <em>Password read</em> Transition, drop the <em>Read password</em> State on the Transition, and in the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Split Transition</strong></span> dialog, choose the <strong>Before Transition</strong> button.</span></span></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:alt="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:height="200" ac:width="160"><ri:attachment ri:filename="state_machine_diagram_2.png" /></ac:image><ac:image ac:title="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:alt="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:height="242" ac:width="163"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Insert_symbol_before_path_with_state.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>After <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Transition </span></strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">button</span>. The shape is inserted before the path. It means the existing path is created, the dropped element symbol is drawn, and then a new path is drawn.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="125c74a4-a702-4fa7-af1e-ad6533f7bf0f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">For example, <em>Name </em><em>read</em> Transition is drawn from <em>Read Name</em> State to <em>Verification</em> State. If you want to insert the <em>Read</em><em> password</em> State after the <em>Name read</em> Transition, drop the <em>Read password</em> State on the Transition, and in the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Split Transition</strong></span> dialog, choose the <strong>After Transition</strong> button. </span></p><ac:image ac:title="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:alt="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:height="222" ac:width="159"><ri:attachment ri:filename="state_machine_diagram_1.png" /></ac:image><ac:image ac:title="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:alt="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:height="252" ac:width="154"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Insert_symbol_after_path_with_state.png" /></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Do Not Insert </strong>button. Action is canceled and the dialog is closed.</span></li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69da0fcd-52d9-42db-9ab9-6f589edb6390"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Split Transition</strong></span> dialog</span>, select the <strong>Remember my choice</strong> check box and the next time the shape will be inserted before or after the path, depending on your selection.</span></p><ac:image ac:title="Split Transition dialog" ac:alt="Split Transition dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="split_transition_dialog.png" /></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong><br /></strong></span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968487 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05525: Inserting images into HTML text

- page_id: `243968487`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968487/Inserting+images+into+HTML+text
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:00.121+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > HTML editor
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: By pasting or dragging rich text with an image

INLINE

To insert an image into HTML text

#### EXPAND: From the Containment tree

From the Containment tree

- When the image is already an AttachedFile, please refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with attached files' space=''] .

#### EXPAND: By using the Advanced HTML Editor

By using the Advanced HTML Editor

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Advanced HTML Editor dialog' space=''] .
2. On the toolbar, click Insert Image button [ATTACHMENT filename='insert_image_button.png'] , then click one of the following:
  - [ATTACHMENT filename='insert_image_button.png'] From File – to browse the image in your file system or paste the attached image file URL into the Picture source field.
  - [ATTACHMENT filename='insert_attached_image.png'] From Attached File – to open the Select Elements dialog and select the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile image should be attached.
  - [IMAGE alt='' src=''] **From Image Library** – to use images from a bundled image collection or your own added sets of images.
3. Click OK twice to close both dialogs.

#### EXPAND: By pasting or dragging the image file from file system

By pasting or dragging the image file from file system

1. Open the image file location, select the file(s) you want to insert, then do one of the following:
  - copy/paste them to the HTML Editor. In the **Paste Special**menu, select **Image**to paste image and attach it to the project or **Image (without attaching)** to paste it only for referencing in the diagram.
  - drag them to the HTML Editor. In the **Select Image Owner**, select the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile image should be attached. If you click **Cancel**, the image will be displayed in the diagram, but not attached to the project.
2. Click**OK**to close the dialogs. If you chose to not attach the image, it is displayed in the HTML Editor, but when you delete the file from your file system, it is no longer displayed. That also means other users you want to share the project with won't be able to see the image if they open the project from another device.This option is very useful when the file you want to insert is large and you don't want to increase the project size. This feature works on both the HTML Editor and Advanced HTML Editor.

#### EXPAND: By pasting or dragging an image from a web browser

By pasting or dragging an image from a web browser

1. Do either:
  - copy/paste the image to the HTML Editor. In the Paste Special menu, select Image to paste image and attach it to the project or HTML Text to paste it only for referencing in the diagram.
  - drag the image to the HTML Editor.
2. Click **OK** to close the dialogs. This feature works only on the HTML Editor. This feature works only with Google Chrome and Mozilla Firefox.

#### EXPAND: By pasting or dragging rich text with an image

By pasting or dragging rich text with an image

1. Do either:
  - copy/paste content to the HTML Editor. In the Paste Special menu, select HTML Text to choose whether paste it only for referencing in the diagram or attach it to the project, or select Image to paste and attach it to the project, or select Plain Text to paste only text without an image.
  - drag the image to the HTML Editor. In the Select Image Owner , select the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile image should be attached. If you click**Cancel**, the image will be displayed in the diagram, but not attached to the project.
2. Click **OK** to close the dialogs. This feature works only on HTML Editor. Only rich text from MS Office files is supported. If the image from the rich text document is not stored in your file system, you are not asked to choose whether to attach the file or not.

To insert multiple image files

1. Open the image files location, select the files you want to insert, then do either:
  - copy/paste them to the HTML Editor. In the Paste Special menu, select Image to paste images and attach them to the project or Image (without attaching) to paste them only for referencing in the diagram.
  - drag them to the HTML Editor. In the Select Image Owner , select the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile images should be attached.
2. Click OK to close the dialogs.

This feature works on both HTML Editor and Advanced HTML Editor.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968606 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05526: Inspecting changes in the Change details panel

- page_id: `243968606`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968606/Inspecting+changes+in+the+Change+details+panel
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:03.874+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Model Merge > Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['change-details', 'merge-result', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1991256206

INLINE

1991256208

INLINE

1991256207

INLINE

The **Change details** panel displays a tree structure reflecting the changes that occurred either on the element selected in the **Merged** **Result** tree/list or on the property selected in the **Specification** panel. The panel is located under the **Specification** panel on the **Merge** window.

The panel contains a tree structure that has two top-level branches – Source changes and Target changes – in 3-way merge, and 1 top-level branch – Source changes – in 2-way merge. In this panel, you can accept or reject changes using the shortcut menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In this panel, you can see dependent changes. In order to see them, expand the **Dependent changes** branch under an element with dependent changes.

For the dependent changes description, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding change concept' space=''].

The Multiplicity attribute is merged as a string, meaning that Lower Value and Upper Value are merged and transformed to numerical/text types accordingly.

1991256204

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Getting merge summary information and changes legend' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding content of the Merged Result panel' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Inspecting element property changes in the Specification panel' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying and navigating through changes' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Quickly navigating through conflicting changes' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Accepting and rejecting changes using shortcut menu' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Viewing and analyzing changes of modified diagrams' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating a differences report' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Getting merge <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969442 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05527: Inspecting differences in the Change details panel

- page_id: `243969442`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969442/Inspecting+differences+in+the+Change+details+panel
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:29.223+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Comparing projects > Analyzing comparison results
- labels: ['change-details', 'differences', 'difference-viewer', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Change details** panel displays a tree structure reflecting the differences between the two versions of either the element selected in the **Differences** tree or the property selected in the **Specification** panel. If several elements or properties are selected, then all changes that have been detected in these elements or properties accordingly will be displayed in the **Change** **details** panel. The panel is located above the **Specification** panel on the **Difference Viewer** window.

The panel contains a tree structure that has 1 top-level branch – Changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In this panel, you can see dependent changes. In order to see them, expand the **Dependent changes** branch under an element with dependent changes.

For the dependent changes description, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding change types' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Change details</strong> panel displays a tree structure reflecting the differences between the two versions of either the element selected in the <strong>Differences</strong> tree or the property selected in the <strong>Specification</strong> panel. If several elements or properties are selected, then all changes that have been detected in these elements or properties accordingly will be displayed in the <strong>Change</strong> <strong>details</strong> panel. The panel is located above the <strong>Specification</strong> panel on the <strong>Difference Viewer</strong> window.</p><p>The panel contains a tree structure that has 1 top-level branch <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="edfa489e-d703-4e81-84d4-4e561ff6619a">– Changes.</ac:inline-comment-marker>   <span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> <br /> </span></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Change details dialog" ac:alt="Change details dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="change_details_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><p>In this panel, you can see dependent changes. In order to see them, expand the <strong>Dependent changes</strong> branch under an element with dependent changes.</p><p>For the dependent changes description, r<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="319a5311-84d4-4531-8f99-20cc6ed2e97e">efer t</ac:inline-comment-marker>o <ac:link ac:anchor="dependentchange"><ri:page ri:content-title="Understanding change types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Dependent changes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969438 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05528: Inspecting element property changes in the differences Specification panel

- page_id: `243969438`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969438/Inspecting+element+property+changes+in+the+differences+Specification+panel
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:23:59.755+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Comparing projects > Analyzing comparison results
- labels: ['property-changes', 'compare-specification-panel', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can see all the details of the element property changes in the **Specification** panel that is located at the bottom of the **Difference Viewer** window. All you need to do is selecting the appropriate element in the **Differences** tree.

As you can see in the following figure, the first column of the panel contains a list of element property names, and other columns display their value changes that have been detected in both base and compared projects.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To navigate from the **Specification** panel to the **Differences** tree, do either

- In the Properties column, right-click the name of a property, which references other elements. Then on its shortcut menu, point to Select in Differences tree and choose an element to which you want to navigate.
- I n the Base or Compared column, right-click the value of a property, which references other elements. Then on its shortcut menu choose an element to which you want to navigate.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
 **Inspecting differences between textual values**

You can see exact changes between textual values of element properties, such as element documentation, comments, pre- and post- conditions of a use case. Differences of compared texts can be displayed in a single dialog, where inserted and deleted parts of the text are appropriately highlighted. As of version 18.3, the differences between compared text written in HTML are now represented as rich text, enabling you to see all changes in formatting as well.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
 To see the differences of compared texts, do either

- Select a property with a modified text on the Specification panel and then click [ATTACHMENT filename='compare_property_values_button.png'] on the toolbar.
- Double-click the property on the Specification panel. The Compare property dialog opens.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968602 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05529: Inspecting element property changes in the Specification panel

- page_id: `243968602`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968602/Inspecting+element+property+changes+in+the+Specification+panel
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:23:32.128+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Model Merge > Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['merge-specification-panel', 'property-changes', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1086956613

1086956615

1086956614

You can see all the details of the element property changes in the **Specification** panel that is located at the top right of the **Merge** window. All you need to do is selecting the element in the **Merged Result** tree or list.

As you can see in the following figure, the first column of the panel contains a list of element property names, and other columns display their value changes that have been detected in both source and the and target. Third column Merge Results Preview shows the final merge result. Properties changes can be accepted or rejected in this panel.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To navigate from the **Specification** panel to the **Merged Result** tree, do either

- In the Properties column, right-click the name of a property, which references other elements. Then on its shortcut menu, point to Select in Merged Result Tree and choose an element to which you want to navigate.
- In the Source , Ancestor , Target or Merge Result Preview column, right-click the value of a property, which references other elements. Then on its shortcut menu choose an element to which you want to navigate.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The **Ancestor** column by default is hidden. To show it in the **Specification**panel, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the **Merge**toolbar and select **Show Ancestor**.

The Multiplicity attribute is merged as a string, meaning that Lower Value and Upper Value are merged and transformed into numerical/text types accordingly.

**Inspecting differences between textual values**

You can see exact changes between textual values of element properties, such as element documentation, comments, pre- and post-conditions of a use case. Differences of compared texts can be displayed in a single dialog, where inserted and deleted parts of the text are highlighted.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
To see the differences of compared texts, do either

- Select a property with a modified text on the Specification panel and then click [ATTACHMENT filename='compare_property_values_button.png'] on the toolbar.
- Double-click the property on the Specification panel. The Compare property dialog opens.

1086956612
