<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968131 space=MT version=6 -->
## PAGE 05613: Managing Map Groups

- page_id: `243968131`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968131/Managing+Map+Groups
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-09-10T10:17:28.614+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Importing data from Excel and CSV files
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The purpose of the Map Groups is to simplify the import of Import Maps that depend on each other. By using the Map Groups you avoid having to manually import multiple maps individually. You can collect saved Import Maps in map Group(s) to upload them one by one automatically when importing the Map Group. Before importing data by using an existing Map Group, you need to create them first and then add saved Import Maps. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving an Import Map' space='']>]]>

It is useful to create a Map Group only if it contains at least two Import Maps.

The following figure demonstrates the *CarBreak Structure Satisfy Requirements*Map Group that contains *CarBreak Requirments*, *CarBreak Structure* and *Car Break Satisfy*Import Maps. When importing this Map Group, the Import Maps are executed in the same order as specified in the **Manage Map Groups** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The *CarBreak Structure Satisfy Requirements*Map Group in the **Manage Map Groups** dialog.

##### Creating Map Groups

To create a Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Manage Map Groups .
2. In the **Tree** tab of the **Manage Maps Groups** dialog, select the *Excel/CSV imports*Package as the owner for the new Map Group.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The Specification window opens. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the **Name** property value, type the name for the new Map Group and click the **Close** button.

The map group is created and becomes visible in the **Select Map Group for Editing**drop-down list box of the **Manage Maps Groups** dialog. Now you can add Import Maps to the newly created Map Group.

##### Deleting Map Groups

To delete the Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Manage Map Groups .
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Select Map Group for Editing option and select the Map Group.
3. Click the **Delete**button. The Map Group is deleted.

##### Adding import maps to a Map Group

To add Import Maps to a Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Manage Map Groups . The **Manage Maps Groups** dialog opens.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Select Map Group for Editing option and select the Map Group.
3. From the Tree or List tab, select the Import Map you want to add to the selected Map Group.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']to add only the selected Import Map or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to add it recursively.

The Import Map(s) are added to the Map Group

##### Removing import maps from a Map Group

To remove an Import Map from a Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Manage Map Groups . The**Manage Maps Groups**dialog opens.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Select Map Group for Editing option and select the Map Group.
3. In the **Map Groups** area, select the Import Map you want to remove.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to remove only the selected Import Map or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to remove them recursively.

The Import Map(s) are removed from the Map Group.

##### Reordering import maps in the Map Group

By specifying the order of the Import Maps in the Map Group, you define the order of their import.

To reorder the Import Maps in the Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Manage Map Groups . The **Manage Maps Groups** dialog opens.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Select Map Group for Editing option and select the Map Group.
3. In the **Map Groups** area, select the Import Map you want to reorder.
4. Click **Up** to move the selected Import Map up or click **Down** to move the Import Map down.

The Import Map(s) are reordered in the selected Map Group.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing data from Excel and CSV files' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving an Import Map' space='']
- Excel and CSV Import dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The purpose of the Map Groups is to <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">simplify the import of Import Maps that depend on each other. By using the Map Groups you avoid having to manually import multiple maps individually</span>. You can collect saved Import Maps in map Group(s) to upload them one by one automatically when importing the Map Group. Before importing data by using an existing Map Group, you need to create them first and then add saved Import Maps. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Saving an Import Map" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to save Import Map >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0371a7b-7feb-4cf2-b478-dcb3347cfefc"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is useful to create a Map Group only if it contains at least two Import Maps.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The following figure demonstrates the <em>CarBreak Structure Satisfy Requirements </em>Map Group that contains <em>CarBreak Requirments</em>, <em>CarBreak Structure</em> and <em>Car Break Satisfy </em>Import Maps. When importing this Map Group, the Import Maps are executed in the same order as specified in the <strong>Manage Map Groups</strong> dialog.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="manage_map_group_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The <em>CarBreak Structure Satisfy Requirements </em>Map Group in the <strong>Manage Map Groups</strong> dialog.</h6><h3>Creating Map Groups</h3><p><br /></p><p>To create a Map Group</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="f2a810cd-3e3e-4af9-90c6-81b1f9c380c3"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Manage Map Groups</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></li><li data-uuid="a6fc0556-83bb-43bc-9283-f0fa4fb45eb4"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Tree</strong> tab of the <strong>Manage Maps Groups</strong> dialog, select the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><em>Excel/CSV imports</em> </span>Package as the owner for the new Map Group.</span></p></li><li data-uuid="c4927c38-d351-43a0-9054-0c98e984610b"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_map_group.png" /></ac:image>. T<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">he Specification window opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="map_group_specification.png" /></ac:image></span></span></li><li data-uuid="4a933019-cc48-4563-8ae4-3b2a98d2d2a2"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Name</strong> property value, <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">type the name for the new Map Group and click the <strong>Close</strong> button.</span></span></p></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The map group is created and becomes visible in the <strong>Select Map Group for Editing </strong>drop-down list box of the <strong>Manage Maps Groups</strong> dialog. Now you can add Import Maps to the newly created Map Group.</span></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3>Deleting Map Groups</h3><p><br /></p><p>To delete the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Map Group</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="f60dcc58-12ac-4615-a1d7-29cd58d4ba27"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Manage Map Groups</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></li><li data-uuid="8e25b4f4-59b1-4909-b783-b8b18dad65a5"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png" /></ac:image> next to the </span><strong>Select Map Group for Editing</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> option and select the Map Group.</span></li><li data-uuid="e1fbda9a-bf56-4412-b9d0-8466e682e536"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>Delete </strong>button. <br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Map Group is deleted.</span></li></ol><h3>Adding import maps to a Map Group</h3><p><br /></p><p>To add Import Maps to a Map Group</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="e13639d0-ce18-4f28-8876-fff3cb186c65"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Manage Map Groups</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. The <strong>Manage Maps Groups</strong> dialog opens.</span></li><li data-uuid="e83d5014-2844-4eda-b3c8-59fd8e9b3206"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png" /></ac:image> next to the </span><strong>Select Map Group for Editing</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> option and select the Map Group.</span></li><li data-uuid="5773f120-3bd6-48a0-a1bb-6180f27ac66f"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">From the Tree or List tab, select the Import Map you want to add to the selected Map Group.</span></li><li data-uuid="c1bd9312-13d3-43a1-adfb-abcfec7d62c9"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_import_map.png" /></ac:image>to add only the selected Import Map or click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_recursively_import_map.png" /></ac:image> to add it recursively.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Import Map(s) are added to the Map Group</span></p><h3>Removing import maps from a Map Group</h3><p><br /></p><p>To remove an Import Map from a Map Group</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="0acd57da-dd2a-4cc2-9a05-4ec6aa9b2e5e"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Manage Map Groups</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565228 space=MT version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 05615: Managing project properties

- page_id: `249565228`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565228/Managing+project+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:25:35.160+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures
- labels: ['architectural-description-element', 'root-element', 'manage-project-properties', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All project properties are stored in the Architectural Description element which is also the root element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can view, add, or edit project properties in the Specification window of the Architectural Description.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>All project properties are stored in the Architectural Description element which is also the root element.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Architectural description as the root element" ac:title="Architectural description as the root element"><ri:attachment ri:filename="architectural_description.png" /></ac:image></p><p>You can view, add, or edit project properties in the Specification window of the Architectural Description.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <ac:image ac:alt="Architectural Description Specification window" ac:title="Architectural Description Specification window"><ri:attachment ri:filename="architectural_description_specification.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /> </span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562545 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05616: Managing PV-2 diagram

- page_id: `249562545`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562545/Managing+PV-2+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:47.504+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Project viewpoint > PV-2 Project Timelines
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can manage the data in PV-2 diagram in the following ways:

##### Adding Actual Projects

To add a new Actual Project

1. On the PV-2 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project . An empty line for the newly created Actual Project appears on the diagram pane.
2. Specify a time frame for the Actual Project.

To add an existing Actual Project

1. On the PV-2 diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Actual Project dialog, select an Actual Project (or several Actual Projects) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. An Actual Project appears on the diagram pane.

##### Adding Actual Sub Projects

To add a new Actual Sub Project

1. Select the Actual Project for which you want to create a Actual Sub Project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the PV-2 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Sub Project .
  - Right click Actual Project and select Add new Actual Sub Project .
3. An empty line for the newly created Actual Sub Project appears on the diagram pane.
4. Type its name
5. Specify Start Date and End Date for the Actual Sub Project.

##### Adding Actual Project Milestones

To add an Actual ProjectMilestone in PV-2 diagram

1. Select a project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project **Milestone** .
  - right-click a Project and click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
3. Select one of the available milestone kinds:
  - In Service
  - Deployed
  - No Longer Used
  - Out Of Service
  - Other
4. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard' space='']**Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard** .
5. Click the Finish button when you are done.

##### editingEditing Project Status

To edit the Project Status in PV-2 diagram

1. On the diagram pane, right-click the milestone and select **Edit Status**. If the milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select **Edit Status** and then select the proper milestone for which you want to edit the status. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space=''] step 2 opens.
2. Edit the Project Status in the following way: 
a) Select desired project thread in the Project Thread area. 
b) Define selected project thread in the Status Indicator area.
3. Click **Finish** when you are done.

##### Relating Actual Projects

You can relate projects to a sequence using the following relation types:

- Finish to Start. Target project cannot start earlier than the source project finishes.
- Start to Start. The target project cannot start earlier than the source project starts.
- Finish to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project finishes.
- Start to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project starts.

You can change project dates by moving a project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.

To relate Actual Projects

1. Select two Actual Projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select the relation type:
  - Finish to Start
  - Start to Start
  - Finish to Finish
  - Start to Finish

To remove the relation between Actual Projects

1. Select two related Actual Projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select None .

To resolve the incorrect project sequence

1. Right-click the highlighted relation and click Validation .
2. Select one of the suggested solutions. If you change dates of an Actual Project containing Actual Sub Projects, the Actual Sub Project dates change correspondingly.

##### Deleting Actual Project Milestones

To delete an Actual Project Milestone

- In the diagram pane, right-click an Actual Project Milestone and select Delete .

##### Deleting Actual Projects

To delete a Actual Project from the chart

1. Select a Actual Project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete From Chart button.

To delete a Actual Project from the model

1. Select a Actual Project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

##### removeRemoving Project Status

To remove the Project Status

1. In the diagram pane, right-click an Actual Project Milestone and select Edit Status . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space=''] step 2 opens.
2. In the right bottom corner of the Preview area, click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_project_status_indicating_mark.png'] .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can manage the data in PV-2 diagram in the following ways:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40c34fe0-78a7-4113-b30e-9b351e1978bb" /></p><h3>Adding Actual Projects</h3><p>To add a new Actual Project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the PV-2 diagram toolbar, click<strong> Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Actual Project</strong>. <strong> Actual Project</strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong> Milestone</strong></span>.</li><li>right-click a Project and click <strong>Add new Actual Project Milestone</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Select one of the available milestone kinds:<br /><ul><li>In Service</li><li>Deployed</li><li>No Longer Used</li><li>Out Of Service</li><li>Other</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps of the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Finish</strong> button when you are done.</li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b587912c-1a77-409d-bb04-6b31cdbff20a"><ac:parameter ac:name="">editing</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Editing Project Status</h3><p>To edit the Project Status in PV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li><p>On the diagram pane, right-click the milestone and select <strong>Edit Status</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09bbfbf5-0d29-4034-afb9-5987f3f65b10"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select <strong>Edit Status</strong> and then select the proper milestone for which you want to edit the status.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project Status Application Wizard" /></ac:link> step 2 opens.</p></li><li><p>Edit the Project Status in the following way:<br />a) Select desired project thread in the Project Thread area.<br />b) Define selected project thread in the Status Indicator area.</p></li><li><p class="confluence-link">Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</p></li></ol><h3>Relating Actual Projects</h3><p>You can relate projects to a sequence using the following relation types:</p><ul><li>Finish to Start. Target project cannot start earlier than the source project finishes.</li><li>Start to Start. The target project cannot start earlier than the source project starts.</li><li>Finish to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project finishes.</li><li>Start to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project starts.</li></ul><p>You can change project dates by moving a project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.</p><p> </p><p>To relate Actual Projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two Actual Projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select the relation type:<br /><ul><li><strong>Finish to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Finish to Finish</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Finish</strong></li></ul></li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove the relation between Actual Projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two related Actual Projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select <strong>None</strong>.<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> <br /></span></li></ol><p> </p><p>To resolve the incorrect project sequence<span> </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the highlighted relation and click <strong>Validation</strong>.</li><li><p>Select one of the suggested solutions.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1fb35907-6f21-414e-9f69-ae4a3c73a422"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you change dates of an Actual Project containing Actual Sub Projects, the Actual Sub Project dates change correspondingly.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Deleting Actual Project Milestones</h3><p>To delete an Actual Project Milestone</p><hr /><ul><li>In the diagram pane, right-click an Actual Project Milestone and select <strong>Delete</strong>.</li></ul><h3>Deleting Actual Projects</h3><p>To delete a Actual Project from the chart</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Actual Project.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Delete From Chart</strong> button.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To delete a Actual Project from the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Actual Project.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Delete </strong>button.</li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ccc8194f-b1c8-4e01-8287-cd83bd4f1614"><ac:parameter ac:name="">remove</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Removing Project Status</h3><p>To remove the Project Status</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram pane, right-click an Actual Project Milestone and select <strong>Edit Status</strong>. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project Status Application Wizard" /></ac:link> step 2 opens.</li><li>In the right bottom corner of the Preview area, click <ac:image ac:title="Remove project status indicating mark" ac:alt="Remove project status indicating mark"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_project_status_indicating_mark.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p> </p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565241 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 05618: Managing tables

- page_id: `249565241`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565241/Managing+tables
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:40.065+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures
- labels: ['table-toolbar', 'data-table', 'add-new', 'add-existing', 'delete-from-table', 'delete', 'default-owner', 'move-up', 'move-down', 'show-columns', 'report', 'show-full-types', 'show-rows', 'manage-tables', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

216584849

216584851

216584850

The purpose of the tables is to improve usability of particular UAF products that cannot be achieved by using custom diagrams or matrices. Tables can be created from diagrams menu exactly the same as diagrams. All the tables in UAF plugin are editable.

The table pane consists of two major parts:

1. Toolbar . It's position is fixed at the top of the table.
2. Data Table. It is right below the toolbar.

These parts are arranged horizontally.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Toolbar buttons are quite similar for all tables. There are the **Add****New**, **Add****Existing**, **Delete****from****Table**, **Delete**, **Default****Owner**, **Move****Up** and **Move****Down**, **Show****Columns**, **Report**, **Export**, and **Show****Full****Types** buttons on the toolbar. Other buttons that are specific to particular product are described within the product specification sections.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Add New | To create a new element in the model and a new row in the data table. |
| Add Existing | To allow you choosing one or more existing elements from the model and add a new rows to the data table. |
| Delete from Table | To remove the selected element and row from the data table. |
| Delete | To remove the selected element and row from the data table and the model. |
| Default Owner | To define Constrains and Measurement Sets default ownership. |
| Move Up | To move the selected row up. |
| Move Down | To move the selected row down. |
| Show Columns | To open the shortcut menu with a list of the available columns for the data table.You will find that currently displayed columns are selected.Click an unselected column to display it.Click a selected column to hide it. |
| Report | To create a corresponding spreadsheet report by passing all the elements form the data table directly to the report. The Report button is available for CV-5, OV-3 (DoDAF, MODAF, and NAF), OV-6a (DoDAF, MODAF, and NAF), SV-6, SV-7, SV-10a, SOV-4a, SvcV-7, and SvcV-10a, StV-5 tables. You can generate either an OpenDocument (.ods) or Microsoft Excel 2007 (.xslx) spreadsheet report. |
| Export | To export an active table either to a plain text file (.cvs) or to a Hypertext Markup Language (.html) format. |
| Show Full Types | To show or hide the full path (qualified name) of each element used in the data table. |

Tables are fully synchronized with model which means that modification to the table such as deleting or creating will affect model elements except for a few cases that are described within the product specification sections. 
 
 Some tables, such as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space=''], allow rows filtering. 
 
 To filter row

1. Create an OV-3 or SV-6 table.
2. On the table toolbar, click the Show Rows button.
3. Deselect row types that you want to hide or select row types that you want to show in the table.

216584848

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tables' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966155 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05620: Managing unresolved references

- page_id: `243966155`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966155/Managing+unresolved+references
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:54.659+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Maintaining decomposed model integrity
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1525838642

INLINE

1525838654

INLINE

1526101509

INLINE

**On this page**

3

1525838644

INLINE

An unresolved reference means that your project contains an element that refers to some used project element that has become inaccessible. This can happen because the project element is no longer shared, it was removed, it was moved to a non-shared location, etc. To maintain your model integrity and project composition when the element is inaccessible, the modeling tool recovers the inaccessible elements to maintain the references that are indicated in the project as unresolved.

##### Running composition integrity analysis

If recovered elements are detected, a summary of validation errors is shown in the status line. You can run the composition integrity analysis to find and examine the usages of the recovered elements in your project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A summary of validation errors in the status line.

To run the composition integrity analysis

1. Do either: The **Validation Results** panel showing the recovered elements usages in the project opens in a separate tab. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - composition integrity analysis In the **Active Validation Results** panel, right-click an element that refers to the recovered element and whose abbreviation is REF or NRAPU. From its shortcut menu, select **Run Composition Integrity Analysis**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - composition integrity analysis in Model BrowserIn the Model Browser, right-click an el ement that refers to the recovered element. From its shortcut menu, select Validation > Recovered Element > Run Composition Integrity Analysis . [ATTACHMENT filename='composition_integrity_analysis_from_model_browser.png']
  - On the diagram pane, select the element shape that refers to the recovered element. In the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='smart_manipulator_toolbar_recovered_elements_found.png'] > Run Composition Integrity Analysis . [ATTACHMENT filename='managing_recovered_element_from_smart_manipulator.png']

##### Identifying validation errors

These are the abbreviations of common validation errors and their explanations:

- REF : The element is a recovered element. This element is referenced by another element in the project.
- SRE : T he symbol usage of the recovered element. Diagrams may have more than one recovered element displayed as a symbol. Each symbol of a recovered element is listed as a SRE error.
- URE : The element referencing the recovered element.

##### Handling recovered elements

It is recommended that you eliminate recovered elements from your model, using one of the following methods:

- Eliminate the causes of the element recovery . This is the case when recovered elements still exist. Identify the cause of the element recovery and eliminate it by, for example, sharing the package that contains the recovered element, moving the element back from a non-shared location to the shared one, etc.
- Restore recovered elements . This is the case when recovered elements no longer exist but can be restored from the bits of information the modeling tool still has. The restored element has only ID, name, and type.
- Manage the unresolved references that refer to the appropriate recovered elements . This is the case when recovered elements no longer exist and cannot be restored.

The following links outline methods of managing unresolved references using specific commands:

1525838641

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='']

1574919148

INLINE

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures on this page is the *[ATTACHMENT filename='Project Decomposition Description.mdzip']*****sample model that uses the following projects: *[ATTACHMENT filename='Analysis and Design.mdzip'],**[ATTACHMENT filename='Code Level.mdzip'],* and***[ATTACHMENT filename='domain.mdzip'] .*

Alternatively, you can open the model from*<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\product features\project decomposition .*

from </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\product features\project decomposition .</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br ````
